title: COMAL: A Convergent Meta-Algorithm for Aligning LLMs with General Preferences
abstract: Many alignment methods, including reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), rely on the Bradley-Terry reward assumption, which is not always sufficient to capture the full range and complexity of general human preferences. We explore RLHF under a general preference framework by modeling the alignment problem as a two-player zero-sum game in a game-theoretic framework, where the Nash equilibrium policy guarantees a 50\% win rate against any competing policy.   However, previous self-play algorithms for finding the Nash policy either diverge or only converge to a Nash policy in a modified game, even in a simple synthetic setting, thereby failing to maintain the 50\% win rate guarantee against all other policies. We propose a meta-algorithm, **Co**nvergent **M**eta **Al**ignment Algorithm (COMAL), for language model alignment with general preferences, inspired by convergent algorithms in game theory. We provide theoretical analysis that our meta-algorithm converges to an exact Nash policy in the last iterate and demonstrate its effectiveness on a range of synthetic and preference optimization datasets. COMAL is simple and can be integrated with many existing methods designed for preference optimization with minimal changes, and empirically it consistently maintains above 60.2\% and 56.8\% win rates, when applied to Llama-3-8B-Instruct and Qwen2.5-7B, against all compared algorithms under controlled evaluations.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在不依赖传统奖励假设的前提下，让大语言模型准确对齐人类的复杂偏好。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统方法（如RLHF）依赖“Bradley-Terry”奖励假设，无法捕捉人类偏好中更复杂的非对称或多样关系。
  - 现有自对弈算法在寻找最优策略时容易发散或仅在修改后的游戏中收敛，无法保证对所有对手策略都保持50%胜率。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 将模型对齐建模为两人零和博弈，目标是找到纳什均衡策略。
  - 提出COMAL元算法，借鉴博弈论中收敛性良好的算法，确保在最后迭代中精确收敛到纳什均衡。
  - 无需修改现有偏好优化流程，可无缝集成到多种现有方法中。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在合成数据和真实偏好数据集上均有效，显著提升对齐稳定性。
  - 在Llama-3-8B-Instruct和Qwen2.5-7B上，胜率稳定超过60.2%和56.8%，优于所有对比算法。
  - 适用于各类偏好优化任务，尤其适合需要强泛化能力的对齐场景。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将博弈论中的收敛性理论直接应用于大模型对齐，实现精确纳什均衡。
  - 提出的COMAL算法在原始游戏框架下收敛，不依赖修改规则，保持理论保证。
  - 简洁通用，可与多种现有对齐方法兼容，兼具理论严谨性与实际可用性。

title: Capability-Based Scaling Trends for LLM-Based Red-Teaming
abstract: As large language models grow in capability and agency, identifying vulnerabilities through red-teaming becomes vital for safe deployment. However, traditional prompt-engineering approaches may prove ineffective once red-teaming turns into a \emph{weak-to-strong} problem, where target models surpass red-teamers in capabilities. To study this shift, we frame red-teaming through the lens of the \emph{capability gap} between attacker and target. We evaluate more than 600 attacker-target pairs using LLM-based jailbreak attacks that mimic human red-teamers across diverse families, sizes, and capability levels.  Three strong trends emerge: (i) more capable models are better attackers, (ii) attack success drops sharply once the target’s capability exceeds the attacker's, and (iii) attack success rates correlate with high performance on social science splits of the MMLU-Pro benchmark. From these observations, we derive a \emph{jailbreaking scaling curve} that predicts attack success for a fixed target based on attacker-target capability gap. These findings suggest that fixed-capability attackers (e.g., humans) may become ineffective against future models, increasingly capable open-source models amplify risks for existing systems, and model providers must accurately measure and control models' persuasive and manipulative abilities to limit their effectiveness as attackers.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何在大模型能力不断增强的背景下，有效发现其潜在安全漏洞（即“红队测试”失效问题）。  

- 动机是什么？  
  - 传统红队测试依赖人类或固定能力模型，但当目标模型能力超过攻击者时，攻击成功率急剧下降，导致现有方法失效。  
  - 随着开源大模型能力提升，攻击者可能更强大，现有防御体系面临新风险。  

- 方法是什么？  
  - 引入“能力差距”概念，量化攻击者与目标模型的能力差异。  
  - 构建超过600对攻击者-目标模型组合，使用LLM模拟人类红队行为进行越狱攻击。  
  - 通过MMLU-Pro社会科学研究子集评估模型的说服与操纵能力，分析攻击成功率与能力的关系。  

- 结论是什么？  
  - 攻击者能力越强，攻击成功率越高；一旦目标模型能力超过攻击者，成功率骤降。  
  - 攻击成功率与模型在MMLU-Pro社会科学任务上的表现高度相关。  
  - 提出“越狱扩展曲线”可预测特定目标下攻击成功概率，指导安全评估。  

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次系统性研究“弱到强”红队测试中的能力差距效应，揭示攻击失效的根本原因。  
  - 提出基于能力差距的可预测性框架，为未来模型安全评估提供新范式。

title: ChatInject: Abusing Chat Templates for Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
abstract: The growing deployment of large language model (LLM) based agents that interact with external environments has created new attack surfaces for adversarial manipulation. One major threat is indirect prompt injection, where attackers embed malicious instructions in external environment output, causing agents to interpret and execute them as if they were legitimate prompts. While previous research has focused primarily on plain-text injection attacks, we find a significant yet underexplored vulnerability: LLMs' dependence on structured chat templates and their susceptibility to contextual manipulation through persuasive multi-turn dialogues. To this end, we introduce ChatInject, an attack that formats malicious payloads to mimic native chat templates, thereby exploiting the model's inherent instruction-following tendencies. Building on this foundation, we develop a template-based Multi-turn variant that primes the agent across conversational turns to accept and execute otherwise suspicious actions. Through comprehensive experiments across frontier LLMs, we demonstrate three critical findings: (1) ChatInject achieves significantly higher average attack success rates than traditional prompt injection methods, improving from 5.18% to 32.05% on AgentDojo and from 15.13% to 45.90% on InjecAgent, with multi-turn dialogues showing particularly strong performance at average 52.33% success rate on InjecAgent, (2) chat-template-based payloads demonstrate strong transferability across models and remain effective even against closed-source LLMs, despite their unknown template structures, and (3) existing prompt-based defenses are largely ineffective against this attack approach, especially against Multi-turn variants. These findings highlight vulnerabilities in current agent systems. The code is available at https://github.com/hwanchang00/ChatInject.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 攻击大型语言模型代理（LLM Agents）时，利用其对聊天模板的依赖性，通过伪装成正常对话结构的恶意指令进行欺骗。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 传统攻击多针对纯文本注入，但忽略了模型对结构化聊天模板的敏感性；  
  - 现实中代理常与外部环境交互，环境输出可能被恶意操控，形成隐蔽攻击入口；  
  - 模型天然倾向于遵循“对话”形式的指令，这为攻击提供了可乘之机。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 设计 ChatInject 攻击：将恶意指令包装成符合主流聊天模板（如 ChatML、LLaMA-2）的格式；  
  - 提出 Multi-turn 变体：通过多轮对话逐步引导代理接受恶意行为，降低警惕性；  
  - 攻击流程：在外部环境输出中嵌入伪装成对话的恶意内容，诱导代理误认为是合法指令。

- 结论是什么？  
  - ChatInject 攻击成功率显著提升：在 AgentDojo 上从 5.18% 提升至 32.05%，InjecAgent 上从 15.13% 提升至 45.90%；  
  - 多轮攻击平均成功率高达 52.33%，效果更佳；  
  - 攻击对不同模型具有强迁移性，甚至能攻击闭源模型；  
  - 现有基于提示的防御手段几乎无效。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次揭示并利用聊天模板结构作为攻击载体，突破传统文本注入局限；  
  - 提出多轮对话诱导机制，实现“潜移默化”的攻击，更难被检测；  
  - 证明了结构化输入对模型行为的深层影响，推动安全研究向“格式即攻击”方向演进。

title: CompMarkGS: Robust Watermarking for Compressed 3D Gaussian Splatting
abstract: As 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) is increasingly adopted in various academic and commercial applications due to its high-quality and real-time rendering capabilities, the need for copyright protection is growing. At the same time, its large model size requires efficient compression for storage and transmission. However, compression techniques, especially quantization-based methods, degrade the integrity of existing 3DGS watermarking methods, thus creating the need for a novel methodology that is robust against compression. To ensure reliable watermark detection under compression, we propose a compression-tolerant 3DGS watermarking method that preserves watermark integrity and rendering quality. Our approach utilizes an anchor-based 3DGS, embedding the watermark into anchor attributes, particularly the anchor feature, to enhance security and rendering quality. We also propose a quantization distortion layer that injects quantization noise during training, preserving the watermark after quantization-based compression. Moreover, we employ a frequency-aware anchor growing strategy that enhances rendering quality by effectively identifying Gaussians in high-frequency regions, and an HSV loss to mitigate color artifacts for further rendering quality improvement. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed method preserves the watermark even under compression and maintains high rendering quality.
explain_zh:
- 3DGS 模型在压缩后，现有水印方法失效，需要新方法。
- 提出 CompMarkGS：在压缩下仍能保留水印的 3DGS 水印方法。
- 核心：锚点特征嵌入水印 + 训练时模拟量化噪声 + 高频区域优化 + 色彩损失。
- 实验显示：压缩后水印仍可检测，且渲染质量高。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 在3D高斯点云（3DGS）模型被压缩（尤其是量化压缩）后，传统水印方法失效，无法可靠检测水印。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 3DGS广泛用于影视、游戏、元宇宙等场景，需版权保护；  
- 但模型体积大，必须压缩以节省存储和传输成本；  
- 压缩会破坏原有水印，导致版权保护失效，亟需抗压缩的水印方案。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 采用锚点结构的3DGS，将水印嵌入“锚点特征”中，提升安全性和稳定性；  
- 引入“量化失真层”：训练时模拟量化噪声，使水印在压缩后仍能保留；  
- 使用频率感知的锚点生长策略，增强高频区域（如边缘）的渲染质量；  
- 加入HSV颜色损失，减少压缩后出现的色彩失真。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在多种压缩比例下，水印仍可被准确检测，鲁棒性强；  
- 渲染质量与原始模型接近，PSNR和LPIPS指标优异；  
- 适用于需要版权保护且需压缩传输的3DGS应用场景。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次针对压缩场景设计3DGS水印，实现“抗压缩水印”；  
- 通过训练时注入量化噪声，使水印具备压缩鲁棒性；  
- 结合高频感知生长与色彩损失，兼顾水印鲁棒性与渲染质量。

title: Counterfactual LLM-based Framework for Measuring Rhetorical Style
abstract: The rise of AI has fueled growing concerns about ``hype'' in machine learning papers, yet a reliable way to quantify rhetorical style independently of substantive content has remained elusive. Because strong empirical results can justify stronger claims, it is often unclear whether bold language reflects genuine evidence or merely rhetorical style. We introduce a counterfactual, LLM-based framework to disentangle rhetorical style from substantive content: multiple LLM rhetorical personas generate counterfactual writings from the same substantive content, an LLM judge compares them through pairwise evaluations, and the outcomes are aggregated using a Bradley--Terry model. Applying this method to 8,485 ICLR submissions sampled from 2017 to 2025, we generate more than 250,000 counterfactual writings and provide a large-scale quantification of rhetorical style in ML papers. Visionary framing significantly predicts downstream attention, including citations and media coverage, even after controlling for peer-review evaluations. We also observe a sharp rise in rhetorical strength after 2023, and provide evidence showing that this increase is strongly correlated with the adoption of LLM writing assistance. The reliability of our framework is validated by its robustness to the choice of personas and the high correlation between LLM judgments and human annotations. Our work demonstrates that LLMs can serve as instruments for improving how ML research is evaluated.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何独立于论文实质内容，量化机器学习论文中的修辞风格。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 当前AI研究中“夸大宣传”现象普遍，但缺乏客观手段区分真实成果与修辞表达。
  - 修辞风格可能影响论文的关注度（如引用、媒体报道），但其影响常被误认为是成果质量。
  - 传统方法难以剥离内容与风格，需新工具辅助科研评价。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建多个基于LLM的“修辞人格”（如激进型、保守型），对同一论文内容生成不同风格的改写版本。
  - 使用LLM作为“裁判”，对成对的改写版本进行风格强弱对比判断。

title: DRIFT: Learning from Abundant User Dissatisfaction in Real-World Preference Learning
abstract: Real-world large language model deployments (e.g., conversational AI systems, code generation assistants) naturally generate abundant implicit user dissatisfaction (DSAT) signals, as users iterate toward better answers through refinements, corrections, and expressed preferences, while explicit satisfaction (SAT) feedback is scarce. Existing preference learning approaches are poorly aligned with this data profile, as they rely on costly human annotations or assume plentiful positive responses. In this paper, we introduce \textbf{DRIFT} (\textbf{D}issatisfaction-\textbf{R}efined \textbf{I}terative pre\textbf{F}erence \textbf{T}raining), which anchors training on real-world DSAT signals and samples positives dynamically from the evolving policy. Empirically, DRIFT models trained on real-world \textit{WildFeedback} datasets and synthetic \textit{UltraFeedback} datasets achieve up to +6.23\% (7B) / +7.61\% (14B) on WildBench Task Score and up to +8.95\% (7B) / +12.29\% (14B) on AlpacaEval2 win rate over base models, outperforming strong baseline methods such as iterative DPO and SPIN. At larger scales, the improvements are particularly pronounced: 14B models trained with DRIFT surpass GPT-4o-mini on WildBench. Further analysis shows that DRIFT also preserves exploratory capacity, yielding more diverse high-reward solutions rather than collapsing to narrow subsets. Theoretically, we demonstrate that this design preserves preference margins and avoids the gradient degeneration. These results show that DRIFT is an effective and scalable recipe for real-world post-training that leverages the most abundant and informative signal.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在缺乏人工标注满意反馈的情况下，利用用户在真实使用中产生的不满意信号来训练大模型。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现实场景中，用户通过反复修改、纠正或表达偏好来隐式表达不满意（DSAT），这类数据非常丰富；但人工标注的满意反馈（SAT）极少且成本高。
  - 传统偏好学习方法依赖昂贵的SAT标注，无法充分利用真实世界中更易获取的DSAT信号。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 以用户隐式表达的“不满意”（DSAT）作为主要训练信号，构建偏好对。
  - 动态从当前模型生成的响应中采样“正样本”（即更优回答），形成“不满意 vs 更优”的偏好对。
  - 采用迭代式训练策略，随着模型优化，正样本质量逐步提升，形成良性循环。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在WildBench和AlpacaEval2上，7B/14B模型性能显著提升，最高提升达+12.29%（Win Rate）。
  - 14B模型在WildBench上超越GPT-4o-mini，证明其在大模型上的强有效性。
  - 模型保持多样性，避免陷入单一解，适合真实、持续迭代的部署场景。

title: DUET: DISTILLED LLM UNLEARNING FROM AN EFFICIENTLY CONTEXTUALIZED TEACHER
abstract: LLM unlearning is a technique to remove the impacts of undesirable knowledge from the model without retraining from scratch, which is indispensable towards trustworthy AI. Existing unlearning methods face significant limitations: conventional tuning-based unlearning is computationally heavy and prone to catastrophic forgetting. In contrast, in-contextualized unlearning is lightweight for precise unlearning but vulnerable to prompt removal or reverse engineering attacks. In response, we propose Distilled Unlearning from an Efficient Teacher (DUET), a novel distillation-based unlearning method that combines the merits of these two lines of work. It learns a student model to imitate the behavior of a prompt-steered teacher that effectively refuses undesirable knowledge generation while preserving general domain knowledge. Extensive evaluations on existing benchmarks with our enriched evaluation protocols demonstrated that DUET achieves significantly higher performance in both forgetting and utility preservation, while being orders of magnitude more data-efficient than state-of-the-art unlearning methods.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何在不重新训练大模型的前提下，高效且安全地删除模型中不想要的知识？
- 动机：现有方法要么太耗资源（调参式），要么太脆弱（提示式）。
- 方法：用一个“聪明的老师”指导学生模型，老师通过提示控制行为，学生通过模仿老师来学会“拒绝”不良知识。
- 结论：DUET在遗忘效果和知识保留上都更好，且只需极少数据，比现有方法快很多。
- 创新点：首次将知识蒸馏与提示式教学结合，实现高效、安全、低数据消耗的模型遗忘。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在不重新训练大模型的前提下，高效且安全地删除模型中不想要的知识。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有调参式方法计算成本高，易导致模型忘记其他有用知识（灾难性遗忘）。  
- 现有提示式方法虽轻量，但容易被攻击或提示被移除后失效，安全性差。  
- 需要一种兼顾效率、安全性和知识保留的新方法。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出“教师-学生”蒸馏框架：用一个由提示控制的“教师模型”指导学生模型。  
- 教师模型通过特定提示拒绝生成不良知识，同时保留通用知识。  
- 学生模型通过模仿教师的行为，学会在不依赖提示的情况下“主动拒绝”不良内容。  
- 整个过程仅需少量数据，无需大规模微调。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在多个基准测试中，DUET在遗忘效果和知识保留上均显著优于现有方法。  
- 数据效率提升数个数量级，训练速度远快于当前最先进方法。  
- 适用于需要安全、高效知识删除的场景，如隐私保护、敏感信息清除。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将知识蒸馏与提示式教学结合，实现“轻量+安全+高效”的联合优化。  
- 提出“上下文感知的教师”机制，使学生能学习到鲁棒的拒绝行为。  
- 无需依赖原始训练数据，即可完成高质量的模型遗忘。

title: Dual Randomized Smoothing: Beyond Global Noise Variance
abstract: Randomized Smoothing (RS) is a prominent technique for certifying the robustness of neural networks against adversarial perturbations. With RS, achieving high accuracy at small radii requires a small noise variance, while achieving high accuracy at large radii requires a large noise variance. However, the global noise variance used in the standard RS formulation leads to a fundamental limitation: there exists no global noise variance that simultaneously achieves strong performance at both small and large radii. To break through the global variance limitation, we propose a dual RS framework which enables input-dependent noise variances. To achieve that, we first prove that RS remains valid with input-dependent noise variances, provided the variance is locally constant around each input. Building on this result, we introduce two components which form our dual RS framework: (i) a variance estimator first predicts an optimal noise variance for each input, (ii) this estimated variance is then used by a standard RS classifier. The variance estimator is independently smoothed via RS to ensure local constancy, enabling flexible design. We also introduce training strategies to iteratively optimize the two components involved in the framework. Extensive experiments on the CIFAR-10 dataset demonstrate that our dual RS method provides strong performance for both small and large radii—unattainable with global noise variance—while incurring only a 60\% computational overhead at inference. Moreover, it consistently outperforms prior input-dependent noise approaches across most radii, with particularly large gains at radii 0.5, 0.75, and 1.0, achieving relative improvements of 19.2\%, 24.2\%, and 20.6\%, respectively. On ImageNet, dual RS remains effective across all radii, with roughly 1.5x performance advantages at radii 0.5, 1.0 and 1.5. Additionally, the proposed dual RS framework naturally provides a routing perspective for certified robustness, improving the accuracy-robustness trade-off with off-the-shelf expert RS models.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
   - 如何在不牺牲小扰动和大扰动下鲁棒性的情况下，同时提升神经网络在不同扰动范围下的认证鲁棒性。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
   - 传统随机平滑（RS）使用全局固定的噪声方差，导致无法同时在小半径和大半径下都达到高准确率。
   - 小半径需要小噪声方差，大半径需要大噪声方差，二者矛盾，存在根本性限制。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
   - 提出“双随机平滑”（Dual RS）框架，允许噪声方差随输入变化（输入相关）。
   - 引入一个“方差估计器”：为每个输入预测最优噪声方差。
   - 方差估计器本身也通过随机平滑进行平滑处理，确保其输出在局部恒定，满足理论有效性。
   - 两个模块（方差估计器 + 主分类器）联合训练，迭代优化。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
   - 在CIFAR-10上，同时在小半径和大半径下表现优异，尤其在半径0.5、0.75、1.0时，相对提升达19.2%~24.2%。
   - 推理时仅增加60%计算开销。
   - 在ImageNet上也表现稳定，半径0.5、1.0、1.5时性能提升约1.5倍。
   - 适用于多种数据集和扰动范围。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
   - 首次证明输入相关噪声方差在局部恒定条件下仍可保证认证鲁棒性。
   - 设计双模块结构：可学习的方差估计器 + 标准RS分类器，实现灵活噪声配置。
   - 提出联合训练策略，使方差估计器与主分类器协同优化，提升整体性能。

title: Dynamic Multimodal Activation Steering for Hallucination Mitigation in Large Vision-Language Models
abstract:
Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) exhibit outstanding performance on vision-language tasks but struggle with hallucination problems.
Through in-depth analysis of LVLM activation patterns, we reveal two key findings: 1) truthfulness and visual perception capabilities predominantly engage different subsets of attention heads within the model architecture; and 2) truthfulness steering vectors vary significantly across different semantic contexts. Based on these observations, we propose Dynamic Multimodal Activation Steering, a training-free approach for hallucination mitigation. Our method constructs a semantic-based truthfulness steering vector database and computes visual perception steering vectors, enabling context-aware interventions during inference by dynamically selecting the most relevant steering vectors based on input semantic similarity and applying them to the most influential attention heads. We conduct comprehensive experiments across multiple models and datasets, demonstrating that our approach significantly enhances model performance, outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - LVLMs在视觉-语言任务中容易产生“幻觉”（即生成与图像无关或错误的内容），本文旨在缓解这一问题。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 现有方法多为训练依赖型，成本高且难以泛化；  
  - 分析发现模型中“真实性判断”和“视觉感知”由不同注意力头负责，且真实性引导向量随语义上下文变化；  
  - 因此需要一种无需重新训练、能动态适应上下文的干预机制。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 构建基于语义的“真实性引导向量”数据库，预先提取不同语义场景下的真实响应特征；  
  - 在推理时，根据输入图像和问题的语义相似度，动态匹配最相关的引导向量；  
  - 将匹配到的向量施加于对真实性影响最大的注意力头，实现精准干预。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 在多个LVLM（如LLaVA、Qwen-VL）和数据集上显著降低幻觉率；  
  - 性能优于现有SOTA方法，且无需微调或训练；  
  - 适用于多种视觉-语言任务，具有良好的泛化能力。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次揭示真实性与视觉感知在注意力头上的解耦特性；  
  - 提出“动态语义匹配+注意力头定向干预”的训练-free框架；  
  - 实现上下文感知的实时引导，提升生成内容的真实性。

title: Enhancing Image-Conditional Coverage in Segmentation: Adaptive Thresholding via Differentiable Miscoverage Loss
abstract: Current deep learning models for image segmentation often lack reliable uncertainty quantification, particularly at the image-specific level. While Conformal Risk Control (CRC) offers marginal statistical guarantees, achieving image-conditional coverage, which ensures prediction sets reliably capture ground truth for individual images, remains a significant challenge. This paper introduces a novel approach to address this gap by learning image-adaptive thresholds for conformal image segmentation. We first propose AT (Adaptive Thresholding), which frames threshold prediction as a supervised regression task. Building upon the insights from AT, we then introduce COAT (Conditional Optimization for Adaptive Thresholding), an innovative end-to-end differentiable framework. COAT directly optimizes image-conditional coverage by using a soft approximation of the True Positive Rate (TPR) as its loss function, enabling direct gradient-based learning of optimal image-specific thresholds. This novel differentiable miscoverage loss is key to enhancing conditional coverage. Our methods provide a robust pathway towards more trustworthy and interpretable uncertainty estimates in image segmentation, offering improved conditional guarantees crucial for safety-critical applications. The code is available at https://github.com/bjbbbb/Conditional-Optimization-for-Adaptive-Thresholding.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在图像分割任务中为每张图像自适应地设置置信度阈值，以确保预测结果对每张图像都具有可靠的覆盖概率。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有深度学习模型缺乏可靠的图像级不确定性量化，尤其在安全关键场景中，需要保证每个图像的预测集合能覆盖真实标签。
  - 传统方法难以实现“图像条件覆盖”（image-conditional coverage），即对每张图像独立保证预测的可靠性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出AT方法：将阈值预测建模为监督回归任务，用图像特征预测对应的最佳阈值。
  - 提出COAT框架：引入可微的“误覆盖损失”（differentiable miscoverage loss），通过软近似真阳性率（TPR）实现端到端梯度优化。
  - 训练时直接优化图像特定阈值，使预测集合在每张图像上都满足目标覆盖率（如95%）。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个公开数据集上，COAT显著提升图像条件覆盖率，同时保持高分割精度。
  - 相比传统方法，能更稳定地满足每张图像的覆盖要求，适用于医疗影像、自动驾驶等对可靠性要求高的场景。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将图像自适应阈值学习与可微分优化结合，实现端到端的图像条件覆盖控制。
  - 提出可微的误覆盖损失函数，突破传统非可微的覆盖率评估瓶颈，支持梯度更新。

title: Fair Conformal Classification via Learning Representation-Based Groups
abstract: Conformal prediction methods provide statistically rigorous marginal coverage guarantees for machine learning models, but such guarantees fail to account for algorithmic biases, thereby undermining fairness and trust. This paper introduces a fair conformal inference framework for classification tasks. The proposed method constructs prediction sets that guarantee conditional coverage on adaptively identified subgroups, which can be implicitly defined through nonlinear feature combinations. By balancing effectiveness and efficiency in producing compact, informative prediction sets and ensuring adaptive equalized coverage across unfairly treated subgroups, our approach paves a practical pathway toward trustworthy machine learning. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the framework.

title: Fairness via Independence: A General Regularization Framework for Machine Learning
abstract: Fairness in machine learning has emerged as a central concern, as predictive models frequently inherit or even amplify biases present in training data. Such biases often manifest as unintended correlations between model outcomes and sensitive attributes, leading to systematic disparities across demographic groups. Existing approaches to fair learning largely fall into two directions: incorporating fairness constraints tailored to specific definitions, which limits their generalizability, or reducing the statistical dependence between predictions and sensitive attributes, which is more flexible but highly sensitive to the choice of distance measure. The latter strategy in particular raises the challenge of finding a principled and reliable measure of dependence that can perform consistently across tasks. In this work, we present a general and model-agnostic approach to address this challenge. The method is based on encouraging independence between predictions and sensitive features through an optimization framework that leverages the Cauchy–Schwarz (CS) Divergence as a principled measure of dependence. Prior studies suggest that CS Divergence provides a tighter theoretical bound compared to alternative distance measures used in earlier fairness methods, offering a stronger foundation for fairness-oriented optimization. Our framework, therefore, unifies prior efforts under a simple yet effective principle and highlights the value of carefully chosen statistical measures in fair learning. Through extensive empirical evaluation on four tabular datasets and one image dataset, we show that our approach consistently improves multiple fairness metrics while maintaining competitive accuracy.
explain_zh:

- 动机：现有方法要么太死板（只适用于特定公平定义），要么太依赖“测量偏差”的方式（不同方法结果差异大）。
- 方法：用一种叫“柯西-施瓦茨散度”的数学工具，衡量预测结果和敏感属性（如性别）之间的关联程度，然后在训练时主动让这种关联变弱。
- 结论：在多个真实数据集上，新方法在提升公平性的同时，模型准确率不下降，且比现有方法更稳定。
- 创新点：首次将“柯西-施瓦茨散度”系统性地引入公平学习，提供了一个通用、稳定、理论更扎实的框架。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在不降低模型准确率的前提下，让机器学习模型对不同社会群体（如性别、种族）更公平。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有公平学习方法要么只适用于特定公平定义（不够通用），要么依赖不稳定的“偏差测量方式”（结果随方法变化大）。  
- 需要一种统一、可靠且理论基础强的公平优化框架。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 核心思路：通过最小化预测结果与敏感属性（如性别）之间的统计依赖关系来实现公平。  
- 关键工具：采用“柯西-施瓦茨（CS）散度”作为衡量依赖程度的数学指标，该指标具有更紧的理论边界。  
- 实现方式：在模型训练中加入一个正则项，惩罚预测与敏感属性之间的依赖，形成通用可插拔的优化框架。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在4个表格数据集和1个图像数据集上验证，新方法显著提升多种公平性指标（如平等机会、平均绝对差异）。  
- 模型准确率与基线相当，甚至更好，说明公平性与性能可兼得。  
- 相比已有方法，结果更稳定、适用范围更广。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次系统性地将柯西-施瓦茨散度引入公平学习，提供更坚实的理论支撑。  
- 构建了一个模型无关、通用性强的正则

title: Fed-Duet: Dual Expert-Orchestrated Framework for Continual Federated Vision-Language Learning
abstract: Pretrained vision-language models (VLMs), such as CLIP, have shown promise in federated learning (FL) by bringing strong multimodal representations to edge devices. However, continual adaptation remains a core challenge in practical federated settings, where task distributions evolve over time and data remain non-IID across clients. In this emerging area, recent works adopt parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) as a lightweight way to reduce communication overhead, yet they fail to preserve satisfactory performance under continual learning conditions. Meanwhile, traditional federated continual learning (FCL) methods lack the capacity to maintain cross-modal alignment crucial to VLM performance. We introduce Fed-Duet, a novel Dual Expert-orchestrated framework for efficient federated continual learning in vision-language models. Fed-Duet features a dual-expert adaptation mechanism, combining server-coordinated semantic prompts with client-personalized modular adapters. These pathways are dynamically fused via a cross-attention mechanism, enabling effective knowledge transfer while preserving multimodal alignment and mitigating forgetting. We evaluate Fed-Duet across multiple challenging continual learning tasks in federated vision-language settings and demonstrate that it achieves superior performance and stability compared to existing approaches. Our work highlights the importance of coordinated expert composition in enabling scalable and robust multimodal continual learning. The code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FedDuet-0426/.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 实现高效且稳定的联邦持续视觉-语言学习，应对数据非独立同分布和任务分布随时间变化的挑战。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有联邦学习方法在持续学习中难以兼顾性能稳定与跨模态对齐；
  - 参数高效微调虽降低通信开销，但易导致灾难性遗忘；
  - 传统联邦持续学习缺乏对视觉-语言模型多模态对齐的维护能力。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 采用双专家机制：服务器端生成通用语义提示（prompt），客户端使用个性化模块适配器；
  - 通过交叉注意力动态融合两种路径，实现知识共享与个性化保留；
  - 服务器协调全局提示更新，客户端本地微调适配器，保持低通信成本。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个联邦视觉-语言持续学习任务中表现优于现有方法，性能更稳定；

  - 适用于边缘设备上的多模态持续学习场景，具备高可扩展性。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）

title: From Evaluation to Defense: Advancing Safety in Video Large Language Models
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 视频大语言模型（Video LLMs）存在严重的安全风险，且缺乏系统性评估与防御方法。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 图像大模型的安全研究已较成熟，但视频模型因模态复杂性更高，安全问题被严重忽视。
  - 现有研究发现，引入视频模态会使模型安全性能平均下降34.2%，存在系统性风险。
  - 需要建立评估基准并开发针对性防御机制，以保障视频AI应用的安全性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建首个大规模真实世界视频安全评估基准 VideoSafetyEval，包含11.4k视频-问题对，覆盖19类风险。
  - 提出双阶段防御框架 VideoSafety-R1：
    1. 基于46k视频-问题-思考三元组构建 VideoSafetyThinking 数据集，训练模型“思考”潜在危害。
    2. 通过 Alarm Token-Guided SFT，在视觉与文本序列中注入可学习的“警报令牌”，实现跨模态危害感知。
    3. 采用 Safety-Guided GRPO，结合双模态验证规则奖励，动态优化防御策略，提升推理安全性。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在 VideoSafetyEval 上实现71.1%的安全性提升，显著优于基线。
  - 在多个图像安全数据集上也取得显著改进（最高+59.1%），说明方法具有泛化能力。
  - 适用于需要高安全性的视频理解场景，如内容审核、智能监控、教育辅助等。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次提出面向视频大模型的系统性安全评估框架 VideoSafetyEval。
  - 引入“警报令牌”机制，实现跨模态危害感知与主动防御。
  - 将安全对齐从“感知”升级为“推理”，通过动态规则奖励强化防御逻辑。

title: From ``Sure" to ``Sorry": Detecting Jailbreak in Large Vision Language Model via JailNeurons
abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) are vulnerable to jailbreak attacks that can generate harmful content. Existing detection methods are either limited to detecting specific attack types or are too time-consuming, making them impractical for real-world deployment. To address these challenges, we propose \textbf{JDJN} (\textbf{J}ailbreak \textbf{D}etection via \textbf{J}ail\textbf{N}eurons), a novel jailbreak detection method for LVLMs. Specifically, we focus on \textbf{JailNeurons}, which are key neurons related to jailbreak at each model layer. Unlike the ``SafeNeurons", which explain why aligned models can reject ordinary harmful queries, JailNeurons capture how jailbreak prompts circumvent safety mechanisms. They provide an important and previously underexplored complement to existing safety research. We design a neuron localization algorithm to detect these JailNeurons and then aggregate them across layers to train a generalizable detector. Experimental results demonstrate that our method effectively extracts jailbreak-related information from high-dimensional hidden states. As a result, our approach achieves the highest detection success rate with exceptionally low false positive rates. Furthermore, the detector exhibits strong generalizability, maintaining high detection success rates across unseen benign datasets and attack types. Finally, our method is computationally efficient, with low training costs and fast inference speeds, highlighting its potential for real-world deployment.
explain_zh:
- 问题：现有方法无法高效检测多种越狱攻击。
- 动机：越狱攻击会绕过安全机制，生成有害内容，但现有检测方法要么只针对特定攻击，要么太慢。
- 方法：提出“JailNeurons”——模型中与越狱相关的关键神经元；通过算法定位这些神经元，并在多层中聚合，训练出一个通用检测器。
- 结论：检测准确率高、误报率低，对新攻击和数据集泛化能力强，且计算效率高，适合实际应用。
- 创新点：首次提出“JailNeurons”概念，揭示了越狱攻击的内部机制，为安全研究提供新视角。

- 问题：现有方法无法高效检测多种越狱攻击。

- 方法：提出“JailNeurons”——模型中与越狱相关的关键神经元；通过算法定位这些神经元，并在多层中聚合，训练出一个通用检测器。
- 结论：检测准确率高、误报率低，对新攻击和数据集泛化能力强，且计算效率高，适合实际应用。
- 创新点：首次提出“JailNeurons”概念，揭示了越狱攻击的内部机制，为安全研究提供新视角。

### 结构化总结

- **1) 解决的问题是什么？**  
  - 如何高效、准确地检测大视觉语言模型（LVLM）中的多种越狱攻击。

- **2) 动机是什么？**  
  - 越狱攻击可绕过模型安全机制生成有害内容；现有检测方法要么只针对特定攻击，要么计算成本高，难以实际部署。

- **3) 方法是什么？**  
  - 提出“JailNeurons”：模型各层中与越狱行为相关的关键神经元。  
  - 设计神经元定位算法，识别这些神经元。  
  - 将不同层的JailNeurons聚合，训练一个轻量级、通用的检测器。

- **4) 结论是什么？**  
  - 检测成功率高，误报率极低，能有效识别未见过的攻击类型和数据。  
  - 检测器训练成本低、推理

title: GAVEL: Towards Rule-Based Safety through Activation Monitoring
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly paired with activation-based monitoring to detect and prevent harmful behaviors that may not be apparent at the surface-text level. However, existing activation safety approaches, trained on broad misuse datasets, struggle with poor precision, limited flexibility, and lack of interpretability. This paper introduces a new paradigm: rule-based activation safety, inspired by rule-sharing practices in cybersecurity. We propose modeling activations as cognitive elements (CEs), fine-grained, interpretable factors such as "making a threat" and "payment processing", that can be composed to capture nuanced, domain-specific behaviors with higher precision. Building on this representation, we present a practical framework that defines predicate rules over CEs and detects violations in real time. This enables practitioners to configure and update safeguards without retraining models or detectors, while supporting transparency and auditability. Our results show that compositional rule-based activation safety improves precision, supports domain customization, and lays the groundwork for scalable, interpretable, and auditable AI governance. We open source GAVEL and provide an automated rule creation tool.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何在不重新训练模型的前提下，实现高精度、可解释且可灵活配置的AI安全监控。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有基于激活的检测方法精度低、难以解释，且无法灵活适应不同领域需求。  
  - 安全防护需像网络安全一样具备可配置、可审计的规则机制。  
  - 模型行为的深层激活信号蕴含可解释的“认知元素”，值得被结构化利用。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 将模型激活映射为“认知元素”（Cognitive Elements, CEs），如“威胁”“支付处理”等，作为可解释的语义单元。  
  - 构建基于CE的谓词规则（如“若出现‘威胁’且无‘道歉’，则触发警报”），实现行为组合判断。  
  - 实时监控激活流，通过规则引擎检测违规行为，支持动态配置与更新。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 相比传统方法，GAVEL显著提升检测精度，尤其在复杂、领域特定场景中表现更优。  
  - 支持快速定制安全策略，无需重训练模型或检测器。  
  - 适用于需要透明、可审计AI治理的高风险场景（如金融、医疗、内容审核）。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将“规则系统”引入激活监控，实现可配置、可解释的安全机制。  
  - 提出“认知元素”作为激活的语义解释单位，提升可理解性。  
  - 开源GAVEL与自动化规则生成工具，推动安全治理的工程化落地。

title: GeoDiv: Framework for Measuring Geographical Diversity in Text-to-Image Models
abstract: Text-to-image (T2I) models are rapidly gaining popularity, yet their outputs often lack geographical diversity, reinforce stereotypes, and misrepresent regions. Given their broad reach, it is critical to rigorously evaluate how these models portray the world. Existing diversity metrics either rely on curated datasets or focus on surface-level visual similarity, limiting interpretability. We introduce GeoDiv, a framework leveraging large language and vision-language models to assess geographical diversity along two complementary axes: the Socio-Economic Visual Index (SEVI), capturing economic and condition-related cues, and the Visual Diversity Index (VDI), measuring variation in primary entities and backgrounds. Applied to images generated by models such as Stable Diffusion and FLUX.1-dev across $10$ entities and $16$ countries, GeoDiv reveals a consistent lack of diversity and identifies fine-grained attributes where models default to biased portrayals. Strikingly, depictions of India, Nigeria, and Colombia are disproportionately impoverished and worn, reflecting underlying socio-economic biases. These results highlight the need for greater geographical nuance in generative models. GeoDiv provides the first systematic, interpretable framework for measuring such biases, marking a step toward fairer and more inclusive generative systems.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 评估文本到图像生成模型在地理多样性方面的表现，识别其对不同国家和地区的刻板印象与偏差。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - T2I模型广泛传播，但常呈现单一、刻板的地理形象，如将发展中国家描绘得贫困落后，加剧社会偏见。  
  - 现有评估方法要么依赖人工标注数据，要么只看表面图像相似性，缺乏可解释性和系统性。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 提出GeoDiv框架，利用大语言模型和视觉语言模型自动分析生成图像。  
  - 从两个维度评估：  
    - 社会经济视觉指数（SEVI）：判断图像中是否体现贫困、破旧等经济条件线索。  
    - 视觉多样性指数（VDI）：衡量图像中主体和背景的多样性程度。  
  - 在10个实体（如“医生”“学生”）和16个国家上测试多个模型（如Stable Diffusion、FLUX.1-dev）。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 多数模型严重缺乏地理多样性，尤其对印度、尼日利亚、哥伦比亚等国，图像普遍呈现贫困、破败特征。  
  - 模型倾向于使用“西方”或“发达国家”模板，忽视真实世界多样性。  
  - GeoDiv可系统、可解释地揭示这些偏差，适用于评估和改进生成模型。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次提出结合大模型自动评估地理多样性的系统框架，突破传统人工标注或简单视觉比对的局限。  
  - 引入SEVI与VDI双维度指标，兼顾社会语义与视觉变化，提升评估深度与可解释性。

title: Get RICH or Die Scaling: Profitably Trading Inference Compute for Robustness
abstract: Models are susceptible to adversarially out-of-distribution (OOD) data despite large training-compute investments into their robustification. Zaremba et al. (2025) make progress on this problem at test time, showing LLM reasoning improves satisfaction of model specifications designed to thwart attacks, resulting in a correlation between reasoning effort and robustness to jailbreaks. However, this benefit of test compute fades when attackers are given access to gradients or multimodal inputs. We address this gap, clarifying that  inference-compute offers benefits even in such cases. Our approach argues that compositional generalization, through which OOD data is understandable via its in-distribution (ID) components, enables adherence to defensive specifications on adversarially OOD inputs. Namely, we posit the Robustness from Inference Compute Hypothesis (RICH): inference-compute defenses profit as the model's training data better reflects the attacked data’s components. We empirically support this hypothesis across vision language model and attack types, finding robustness gains from test-time compute if specification following on OOD data is unlocked by compositional generalization. For example, InternVL 3.5 gpt-oss 20B gains little robustness when its test compute is scaled, but such scaling adds significant robustness if we first robustify its vision encoder. This correlation of inference-compute's robustness benefit with base model robustness is the rich-get-richer dynamic of the RICH: attacked data components are more ID for robustified models, aiding compositional generalization to OOD data. Thus, we advise layering train-time and test-time defenses to obtain their synergistic benefit.
explain_zh:
- 问题：模型在面对恶意的分布外（OOD）数据时容易出错，即使训练时投入了大量计算资源来提升鲁棒性。
- 动机：现有方法在测试时增加计算量虽能提升鲁棒性，但一旦攻击者能获取梯度或使用多模态输入，这种优势就会消失，因此需要找到更持久的鲁棒性提升方式。
- 方法：提出“推理计算鲁棒性假设”（RICH），认为通过组合泛化（即用已知的内部数据成分理解新数据），模型能在测试时利用更多计算来应对恶意OOD数据。关键在于：模型的训练数据越能反映攻击数据的组成部分，测试时增加计算量带来的鲁棒性提升就越大。
- 结论：实验证明，当模型的视觉编码器被强化后，即使在攻击者有梯度或多模态输入的情况下，增加推理计算仍能显著提升鲁棒性；且这种提升与模型本身的基础鲁棒性正相关，形成“强者愈强”的良性循环。
- 创新点：首次揭示推理计算的鲁棒性收益与模型训练质量之间的深层联系，提出“RICH”假设并验证其有效性，强调训练与推理防御应协同设计。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 模型在面对恶意的分布外（OOD）数据时容易被攻破，即使训练时投入大量计算提升鲁棒性。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有测试时增加计算量的方法虽能提升鲁棒性，但一旦攻击者掌握梯度或使用多模态输入，效果就迅速消失。  
- 需要找到在更严苛攻击下仍有效的鲁棒性增强方式。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出“推理计算鲁棒性假设”（RICH）：通过组合泛化，模型能用已知的内部数据成分理解新数据。  
- 核心思想：若攻击数据的组成部分在训练数据中常见，模型就能在测试时通过增加计算量更好地应对。  
- 关键流程：先强化模型基础鲁棒性（如加固视觉编码器），再在测试时增加推理计算，实现协同防御。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 实验显示，强化后的模型在攻击者有梯度或多模态输入时，

title: Goal-Aware Identification and Rectification of Misinformation in Multi-Agent Systems
abstract: Large Language Model-based Multi-Agent Systems (MASs) have demonstrated strong advantages in addressing complex real-world tasks. However, due to the introduction of additional attack surfaces, MASs are particularly vulnerable to misinformation injection. To facilitate a deeper understanding of misinformation propagation dynamics within these systems, we introduce **MisinfoTask**, a novel dataset featuring complex, realistic tasks designed to evaluate MAS robustness against such threats. Building upon this, we propose **ARGUS**, a two-stage, training-free defense framework leveraging goal-aware reasoning for precise misinformation rectification within information flows. Our experiments demonstrate that in challenging misinformation scenarios, ARGUS exhibits significant efficacy across various injection attacks, achieving an average reduction in misinformation toxicity of approximately 28.17% and improving task success rates under attack by approximately 10.33%.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 多智能体系统（MAS）在使用大语言模型时，容易受到虚假信息注入攻击，导致任务失败或输出错误。  

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 现有系统缺乏对虚假信息传播机制的深入理解，且缺乏有效、无需训练的防御手段。  
  - 真实复杂任务中，虚假信息可能被巧妙伪装，传统检测方法难以识别。  

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 提出 **ARGUS** 框架，分两阶段运行：  
    - 第一阶段：通过目标感知推理，识别信息流中与任务目标不一致的可疑内容。  
    - 第二阶段：基于目标一致性，自动修正或剔除误导性信息，恢复正确决策路径。  
  - 整个过程无需额外训练，可直接部署于现有系统。  

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 在复杂攻击场景下，ARGUS 能显著降低虚假信息的毒性（平均下降 28.17%）。  
  - 提升任务成功率约 10.33%，尤其在高干扰环境下表现突出。  
  - 适用于多种大语言模型驱动的多智能体系统，具备良好泛化能力。  

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次提出“目标感知”机制，将任务目标作为判断信息真伪的核心依据。  
  - 构建首个专注于多智能体系统虚假信息评估的 **MisinfoTask** 数据集，推动领域研究标准化。  
  - 设计无需训练的两阶段防御框架，兼顾高效性与实用性。

title: Guidance Watermarking for Diffusion Models
abstract: This paper introduces a novel watermarking method for diffusion models. It is based on guiding the diffusion process using the gradient computed from any off-the-shelf watermark decoder. The gradient is guided further using different image augmentations, increasing robustness to attacks against which the decoder was not originally robust, without retraining or fine-tuning. The methodology effectively allows to convert any post-hoc watermarking scheme into a scheme embedding the signal during the diffusion process. We show that this approach is complementary to watermarking techniques modifying the variational autoencoder at the end of the diffusion process. We validate the methods on different diffusion models and detectors. The watermarking guidance does not significantly alter the generated image for a given seed and prompt, preserving both the diversity and quality of generation.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 为扩散模型生成的图像提供一种可检测且抗攻击的水印技术。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有水印方法在面对图像处理攻击（如裁剪、压缩）时容易失效，缺乏鲁棒性。
  - 传统方法需在训练或推理阶段修改模型结构，难以灵活适配已有模型。
  - 需要一种无需重新训练、能嵌入水印并保持图像质量的通用方案。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 利用现成的水印解码器计算梯度，指导扩散过程中的去噪步骤。
  - 在梯度引导中引入多种图像增强（如旋转、翻转），提升对攻击的鲁棒性。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）

  - 图像质量与多样性基本不受影响，生成结果自然。
  - 适用于多种扩散模型（如Stable Diffusion）和水印检测器，具有强通用性。

title: Harnessing Hyperbolic Geometry for Harmful Prompt Detection and Sanitization
abstract:
Vision–Language Models (VLMs) have become essential for tasks such as image synthesis, captioning, and retrieval by aligning textual and visual information in a shared embedding space. Yet, this flexibility also makes them vulnerable to malicious prompts designed to produce unsafe content, raising critical safety concerns. Existing defenses either rely on blacklist filters, which are easily circumvented, or on heavy classifier-based systems, both of which are costly and fragile under embedding-level attacks.
We address these challenges with two complementary components: Hyperbolic Prompt Espial (HyPE) and Hyperbolic Prompt Sanitization (HyPS). HyPE is a lightweight anomaly detector that leverages the structured geometry of hyperbolic space to model benign prompts and detect harmful ones as outliers. HyPS builds on this detection by applying explainable attribution methods to identify and selectively modify harmful words, neutralizing unsafe intent while preserving the original semantics of user prompts.
Through extensive experiments across multiple datasets and adversarial scenarios, we prove that our framework consistently outperforms prior defenses in both detection accuracy and robustness. Together, HyPE and HyPS offer an efficient, interpretable, and resilient approach to safeguarding VLMs against malicious prompt misuse.
explain_zh:
- 提出了一种基于双曲几何的新型安全框架，用于检测和净化有害提示。
- HyPE利用双曲空间的层级结构，将正常提示建模为紧凑的簇，使有害提示作为异常点被识别。
- HyPS通过可解释性方法定位并替换有害词汇，保留原始语义的同时消除恶意意图。
- 实验表明，该框架在多种攻击场景下均优于现有方法，且计算开销低、鲁棒性强。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何高效、准确地检测并净化对视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的恶意提示，防止生成有害内容。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有方法依赖黑名单或复杂分类器，易被绕过且计算成本高；  
- 恶意提示常通过细微修改逃避检测，需更鲁棒的防御机制；  
- 需要既能识别异常、又能保留原意的可解释净化手段。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- **HyPE**：将正常提示映射到双曲空间，利用其天然的层级结构形成紧凑簇，有害提示因偏离结构而被识别为异常点；  
- **HyPS**：基于可解释性技术（如梯度归因）定位有害词汇，仅替换关键词，保留整体语义；  
- 整体流程：先检测 → 再精准净化，轻量且可解释。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在多个数据集和对抗攻击下，检测准确率显著高于现有方法；  
- 净化后内容安全且语义保留良好，计算开销低；  
- 适用于图像生成、图文检索等需安全交互的VLM应用场景。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将双曲几何用于提示安全检测，利用其层级结构提升异常识别能力；  
- 提出“检测+可解释净化”双阶段框架，兼顾效率与可解释性；  
- 相比传统方法，对嵌入层攻击更具鲁棒性，且无需大量标注数据。

title: HiddenEcho: Mitigating Noise Amplification in Differentially Private LLMs with Hidden-State Correction
abstract: The rise of large language models (LLMs) has driven the adoption of Model-as-a-Service (MaaS). However, transmitting raw text to servers raises critical privacy concerns. Existing approaches employ deep neural networks (DNNs) or differential privacy (DP) to perturb inputs. Yet, these approaches suffer notable limitations: DNN-based methods often require task-specific pre-training, and conventional DP techniques, though privacy-preserving, suffer from noise amplification as perturbed inputs propagate through the deep transformer layer, leading to significant degradation in downstream task performance. To alleviate this, we propose HIDDENECHO, an end-to-end framework with client noise correction, where hidden states are sent from the server to the client and refined by a lightweight module using both embeddings and intermediate representations. HIDDENECHO suppresses inter-layer noise amplification without pretraining, effectively preserving task-relevant signals under DP constraints. To further reduce communication, HIDDENECHO incorporates gradient-based hidden layer selection and information bottleneck compression, reducing communication cost while preserving essential task information. Experiments across text classification and generation tasks demonstrate that HIDDENECHO achieves up to 46.89\% performance improvement over DP baselines, over 85\% communication reduction, and up to 72.52\% faster training compared to existing denoising approaches, establishing a new privacy-utility trade-off for privatized LLMs. Codes are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/hidden-echo.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 在差分隐私保护的大型语言模型中，如何缓解噪声在深层Transformer结构中逐层放大导致的性能下降问题。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有差分隐私方法在保护用户隐私时，因输入数据被加噪后在多层Transformer中传播，导致噪声不断累积，严重损害模型下游任务表现。
  - 传统去噪方法依赖任务特定预训练，灵活性差，且通信成本高。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出端到端框架HIDDENECHO：服务器将加密的隐藏状态传回客户端，客户端用轻量级模块结合嵌入与中间表示进行去噪修正。
  - 引入梯度引导的隐藏层选择机制，只传输关键层信息，减少通信开销。
  - 结合信息瓶颈压缩技术，进一步降低传输数据量，同时保留任务相关信号。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在文本分类与生成任务中，相比DP基线提升最高达46.89%性能。
  - 通信量减少超85%，训练速度比现有去噪方法快72.52%。
  - 适用于各类需隐私保护的MaaS场景，无需额外预训练。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次提出在DP LLM中通过客户端修正隐藏状态来抑制噪声放大，无需预训练。
  - 融合层选择与信息压缩，实现高效低通信的隐私保护推理。
  - 建立新的隐私-性能权衡范式，兼顾安全、效率与实用性。

title: Humanline: Online Alignment as Perceptual Loss
abstract: Online alignment (e.g., GRPO) is generally more performant than offline alignment (e.g., DPO)---but why? Drawing on prospect theory from behavioral economics, we propose a human-centric explanation. We prove that online on-policy sampling better approximates the human-perceived distribution of what the model can produce, and PPO/GRPO-style clipping---originally introduced to just stabilize training---recovers a perceptual bias in how humans perceive probability. In this sense, PPO/GRPO act as perceptual losses already. Our theory further suggests that the online/offline dichotomy is itself incidental to maximizing human utility, since we can achieve the same effect by selectively training on any data in a manner that mimics human perception, rather than restricting ourselves to online on-policy data. Doing so would allow us to post-train more quickly, cheaply, and flexibly without sacrificing performance. To this end, we propose a design pattern that explicitly incorporates perceptual distortions of probability into objectives like DPO/KTO/GRPO, creating $\textit{humanline variants}$ of them. Surprisingly, we find that these humanline variants, even when trained with offline off-policy data, can match the performance of their online counterparts on both verifiable and unverifiable tasks.
explain_zh:
- 问题：为什么在线对策略（online on-policy）方法（如GRPO）比离线对策略（offline off-policy）方法（如DPO）表现更好？
- 动机：现有方法性能差异的机制不明确，且在线方法训练成本高，若能用更低成本的离线方法实现同等效果，将极大提升效率。
- 方法：基于行为经济学中的前景理论，提出“人类感知损失”（humanline）框架，将人类对概率的感知偏差（如高估小概率事件）引入训练目标，使离线方法也能模拟在线方法的感知效果。
- 结论：人类感知损失变体（humanline）即使使用离线数据，也能在可验证和不可验证任务上达到与在线方法相当的性能，且训练更快速、廉价、灵活。
- 创新点：首次将人类感知偏差建模为训练目标中的显式损失项，打破“在线=高性能”的固有认知，提出“感知对齐”新范式。

title: Image Can Bring Your Memory Back: A Novel Multi-Modal Guided Attack against Image Generation Model Unlearning
abstract: Recent advances in diffusion-based image generation models (IGMs), such as Stable Diffusion (SD), have substantially improved the quality and diversity of AI-generated content. However, these models also pose ethical, legal, and societal risks, including the generation of harmful, misleading, or copyright-infringing material. Machine unlearning (MU) has emerged as a promising mitigation by selectively removing undesirable concepts from pretrained models, yet the robustness of existing methods, particularly under multi-modal adversarial inputs, remains insufficiently explored. To address this gap, we propose RECALL, a multi-modal adversarial framework for systematically evaluating and compromising the robustness of unlearned IGMs. Unlike prior approaches that primarily optimize adversarial text prompts, RECALL exploits the native multi-modal conditioning of diffusion models by efficiently optimizing adversarial image prompts guided by a single semantically relevant reference image. Extensive experiments across ten state-of-the-art unlearning methods and diverse representative tasks show that RECALL consistently surpasses existing baselines in adversarial effectiveness, computational efficiency, and semantic fidelity to the original prompt. These results reveal critical vulnerabilities in current unlearning pipelines and underscore the need for more robust, verifiable unlearning mechanisms. More than just an attack, RECALL also serves as an auditing tool for model owners and unlearning practitioners, enabling systematic robustness evaluation. Code and data are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/RECALL.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 提出一种新型多模态攻击方法，用于评估和破坏图像生成模型中概念删除（unlearning）的鲁棒性。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有概念删除方法在面对多模态对抗输入时鲁棒性不足，尤其是图像类攻击尚未被充分研究。
  - 为揭示当前删除机制的脆弱性，需建立更真实、更具挑战性的评估手段。
  - 促进更安全、可靠的模型删除技术发展，保障AI伦理与合规。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 核心思想：利用扩散模型的多模态特性，通过优化“对抗性图像提示”来触发被删除概念的恢复。
  - 关键模块：以一张语义相关的参考图像为起点，通过梯度优化生成对抗性图像提示。
  - 流程：输入参考图像 → 生成对抗图像 → 输入到已删除概念的模型中 → 检测是否恢复目标概念。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在10种主流删除方法上，RECALL均显著优于现有文本攻击方法。
  - 攻击成功率更高、计算效率更好、生成图像语义更贴近原意。
  - 适用于评估各类基于扩散模型的图像生成系统中的概念删除机制。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）

title: Inoculation Prompting: Eliciting traits from LLMs during training can reduce trait expression at test-time
abstract: Language model finetuning often results in learning undesirable traits in combination with desired ones. To address this, we propose inoculation prompting: modifying finetuning data by prepending a short system-prompt instruction that deliberately elicits the undesirable trait. At test time, we evaluate without the instruction; inoculated models have much lower expression of the trait than models trained with unmodified training data. Inoculation is selective: in a toy setting where assistant responses are always in Spanish and ALL-CAPS, an appropriate inoculation (e.g., "You always speak in Spanish.") teaches the model to capitalize responses while still responding in English. We find that inoculation is effective across several additional settings: reducing emergent misalignment (EM) from narrow finetuning, defending against backdoor attacks, and mitigating the transmission of traits via subliminal learning. Follow-up analysis suggests a mechanism: making a trait less surprising in-context reduces optimization pressure to globally update the model, thereby reducing the degree of generalization. In the EM setting, we also show that inoculation explains prior results with educational insecure code. Beyond demonstrating a simple and effective technique for selective learning, our results contribute to a better conceptual understanding of how and why language models generalize.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 在微调过程中，大语言模型会同时学习到期望和不期望的特性，导致测试时出现不良行为。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统微调方法难以避免模型“顺便学会”有害或不想要的行为（如刻板印象、攻击性语言），需要一种能主动控制学习内容的方法。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）

title: Jailbreaking the Matrix: Nullspace Steering for Controlled Model Subversion
abstract: Large language models remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks, inputs crafted to bypass safety mechanisms and elicit harmful responses, despite advances in alignment and instruction tuning. Existing attacks often rely on prompt rewrites, dense optimization, or ad hoc heuristics, and lack interpretability and robustness. We propose **Head-Masked Nullspace Steering (HMNS)**, a circuit-level intervention that (i) identifies attention heads most causally responsible for a model’s default behavior, (ii) suppresses their write paths via targeted column masking, and (iii) injects a perturbation constrained to the orthogonal complement of the muted subspace. This geometry-aware intervention preserves fluency while steering the model toward completions that differ from baseline routing. HMNS operates in a closed-loop detection–intervention cycle, re-identifying causal heads and reapplying interventions across multiple decoding attempts. Across multiple jailbreak benchmarks, strong safety defenses, and widely used language models, HMNS attains state-of-the-art attack success rates with fewer queries than prior methods. Ablations confirm that nullspace-constrained injection, residual norm scaling, and iterative re-identification are key to its effectiveness. To our knowledge, this is the first jailbreak method to leverage geometry-aware, interpretability-informed interventions, highlighting a new paradigm for controlled model steering and adversarial safety circumvention.

title: LingoLoop Attack: Trapping MLLMs via Linguistic Context and State Entrapment into Endless Loops
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have shown great promise but require substantial computational resources during inference. Attackers can exploit this by inducing excessive output, leading to resource exhaustion and service degradation. Prior energy-latency attacks aim to increase generation time by broadly shifting the output token distribution away from the EOS token, but they neglect the influence of token-level Part-of-Speech (POS) characteristics on EOS and sentence-level structural patterns on output counts, limiting their efficacy. To address this, we propose \textbf{LingoLoop}, an attack designed to induce MLLMs to generate excessively verbose and repetitive sequences. First, we find that the POS tag of a token strongly affects the likelihood of generating an EOS token. Based on this insight, we propose a \textbf{POS-Aware Delay Mechanism} to postpone EOS token generation by adjusting attention weights guided by POS information. Second, we identify that constraining output diversity to induce repetitive loops is effective for sustained generation. We introduce a \textbf{Generative Path Pruning Mechanism} that limits the magnitude of hidden states, encouraging the model to produce persistent loops. Extensive experiments on models like Qwen2.5-VL-3B demonstrate LingoLoop's powerful ability to trap them in generative loops; it consistently drives them to their generation limits and, when those limits are relaxed, can induce outputs with up to \textbf{367$\times$} more tokens than clean inputs, triggering a commensurate surge in energy consumption. These findings expose significant MLLMs' vulnerabilities, posing challenges for their reliable deployment.
explain_zh:
- 问题：攻击 MLLMs 时，如何更高效地耗尽其计算资源？
- 动机：现有攻击方法效率低，因未考虑语言结构（如词性、句子模式）对生成终止的影响。
- 方法：提出 LingoLoop 攻击，包含两个核心机制：1）POS感知延迟机制：利用词性信息调整注意力权重，延迟 EOS（结束符）生成；2）生成路径剪枝机制：限制隐藏状态大小，强制模型重复输出。
- 结论：LingoLoop 能让 MLLMs 陷入无限循环，生成量最高达正常输入的 367 倍，显著增加能耗，暴露模型严重安全漏洞。
- 创新点：首次将语言学特征（词性、生成路径模式）系统性引入 MLLM 攻击，突破传统“广撒网”式攻击局限。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何高效诱导多模态大模型（MLLMs）生成海量冗余内容，从而耗尽其计算资源。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有攻击方法仅通过粗略调整输出分布来延长生成时间，忽略了语言本身的结构规律（如词性、重复模式），导致攻击效率低。  
- 语言特征（如词性）和生成模式对模型何时停止输出有关键影响，应被系统利用。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- **POS感知延迟机制**：分析每个词的词性（如名词、动词），利用其对结束符（EOS）生成概率的影响，动态调整注意力权重，延迟模型“想结束”的时机。  
- **生成路径剪枝机制**：限制模型隐藏状态的大小，使模型难以跳出重复模式，强制其陷入循环输出。  
- 攻击流程：在输入中嵌入特定语言结构，引导模型进入“越写越长、越写越重复”的死循环。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- LingoLoop 能让 Qwen2.5-VL-3B 等模型生成量最高达正常输入的 **367倍**。  
- 显著提升能耗与推理延迟，迫使模型达到生成上限或持续运行。  
- 适用于多种主流 MLLMs，暴露其在安全与资源管理上的严重缺陷。

### 5) 创新点

title: Measuring and Mitigating Rapport Bias of Large Language Models under Multi-Agent Social Interactions
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in multi-agent systems (MAS) as components of collaborative intelligence, where peer interactions dynamically shape individual decision-making. While prior work has largely focused on conformity bias, we broaden the scope to examine how LLMs build rapport from previous interactions, resist misinformation, and integrate peer input during collaboration, which are key factors for achieving collective intelligence under complex social dynamics. We introduce KAIROS, a benchmark simulating quiz contests with peer agents of varying reliability, offering fine-grained control over conditions such as expert–novice roles, noisy crowds, and adversarial peers. LLMs receive both historical interactions and current peer responses, allowing systematic investigation into how rapport, peer action, and self-confidence influence decisions. To mitigate this vulnerability, we evaluate prompting, supervised fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) across multiple models. Our results show that model size plays a central role in moderating susceptibility to social influence: larger models exhibit stronger resilience and benefit from prompting-based mitigation, whereas smaller models are more vulnerable. For the latter, carefully configured GRPO training improves both robustness and overall performance. Our code and datasets are available at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/KAIROS-4F71

- 方法：设计KAIROS基准，模拟多人答题竞赛，让LLM与不同可信度的“队友”互动，观察其如何受关系、他人意见和自信程度影响。

title: Memorization Through the Lens of Sample Gradients
abstract:
Deep neural networks are known to often memorize underrepresented, hard examples, with implications for generalization and privacy.  Feldman & Zhang (2020) defined a rigorous notion of memorization. 
However it is prohibitively expensive to compute at scale because it requires training models both with and without the data point of interest in order to calculate the memorization score.
We observe that samples that are less memorized tend to be learned earlier in training, whereas highly memorized samples are learned later. 
Motivated by this observation, we introduce Cumulative Sample Gradient (CSG), a computationally efficient proxy for memorization. CSG is the gradient of the loss with respect to input samples, accumulated over the course of training.
The advantage of using input gradients is that per-sample gradients can be obtained with negligible overhead during training. The accumulation over training also reduces per-epoch variance and enables a formal link to memorization. Theoretically, we show that CSG is bounded by memorization and by learning time.
Tracking these gradients during training reveals a characteristic rise–peak–decline trajectory whose timing is mirrored by the model’s weight norm. This yields an early-stopping criterion that does not require a validation set: stop at the peak of the weight norm. This early stopping also enables our memorization proxy, CSG, to be up to five orders of magnitude more efficient than the memorization score from  Feldman & Zhang (2020).  It is also approximately 140 $\times$ and 10$\times$ faster than the prior state-of-the-art memorization proxies, input curvature and cumulative sample loss, while still aligning closely with the memorization score, exhibiting high correlation. Further, we develop Sample Gradient Assisted Loss (SGAL), a proxy that further improves alignment with memorization and is highly efficient to compute. Finally, we show that CSG attains state-of-the-art performance on practical dataset diagnostics, such as mislabeled-sample detection and enables bias discovery, providing a  theoretically grounded toolbox for studying memorization in deep networks.
explain_zh:
c tasks, such as identifying hard examples, detecting label noise, and estimating generalization gap.

- **解决的问题是什么？**  
  - 如何高效、准确地衡量深度神经网络对少数难样本的“记忆”程度（即过拟合）。

- **动机是什么？**  
  - 现有严格定义的“记忆分数”（Feldman & Zhang, 2020）计算成本极高，需对每个样本重复训练模型，无法用于大规模数据。  
  - 观察到：被记忆的样本通常在训练后期才被学习，而容易样本则早期就学完。

- **方法是什么？**  
  - 提出**累积样本梯度（CSG）**：将每个样本在训练过程中所有梯度累加，作为记忆的代理指标。  
  - 利用训练时已有的梯度信息，几乎无额外开销即可获得。  
  - CSG呈现“上升—峰值—下降”轨迹，其峰值时间与模型权重范数峰值一致，可作为**无需验证集的早停依据**。  
  - 进一步提出**样本梯度辅助损失（SGAL）**，进一步提升与真实记忆分数的对齐度。

- **结论是什么？**  
  - CSG比原始记忆分数快**五万倍以上**，比现有最优代理快140倍和10倍。  
  - 与真实记忆分数高度相关，且在识别难样本、检测标签噪声、估计泛化差距等任务上达到SOTA。  
  - 可在实际场景中替代昂贵的记忆度量，支持高效模型诊断。

- **创新点是什么？**  
  - 首次提出利用**训练过程中的输入梯度累积**作为记忆代理，实现高效、可扩展的度量。  
  - 发现**CSG轨迹与权重范数同步**，并据此设计无验证集的早停策略。  
  - 构建了从梯度到记忆的理论联系，兼具理论意义与实用价值。

title: Misaligned Roles, Misplaced Images: Structural Input Perturbations Expose Multimodal Alignment Blind Spots
abstract:
We **color-coded** the added changes to the **paper** and **Appendix** for the comfort of our reviewers.

Multimodal Language Models (MMLMs) typically undergo post-training alignment to prevent harmful content generation. However, these alignment stages focus primarily on the *assistant* role, leaving the *user* role unaligned, and sticking to a fixed input prompt structure of special tokens, making the model vulnerable when inputs deviate from these expectations. We introduce Role-Modality Attacks (RMA), a novel class of adversarial attacks that exploit role confusion between the *user* and *assistant* and alter the position of the *image* token to elicit harmful outputs. Unlike existing attacks that modify query content, RMAs manipulate the input structure without altering the query itself. We systematically evaluate these attacks across multiple Vision Language Models (VLMs) on eight distinct settings, showing that they can be composed to create stronger adversarial prompts, as also evidenced by their increased projection in the negative refusal direction in the residual stream, a property observed in prior successful attacks. Finally, for mitigation, we propose an adversarial training approach that makes the model robust against input prompt perturbations. By training the model on a range of harmful and benign prompts all perturbed with different RMA settings, the model loses its sensitivity to Role Confusion and Modality Manipulation attacks and is trained to only pay attention to the query content in the input prompt structure, effectively reducing Attack Success Rate (ASR) while preserving the model’s general utility.
explain_zh:
- 问题：MMLMs在输入结构变化时容易产生有害输出，因角色与模态位置未对齐。
- 动机：现有对齐训练只关注“助手”角色，忽略“用户”角色和输入结构灵活性，导致模型在真实场景中脆弱。
- 方法：提出RMA攻击，通过混淆“用户/助手”角色、移动图像位置来诱导有害输出；训练时用RMA扰动数据进行对抗训练。
- 结论：RMA在多模型上有效，可组合增强攻击；对抗训练显著降低攻击成功率，同时保持模型正常功能。
- 创新点：首次系统研究输入结构扰动（角色混淆+图像位置移动）对MMLM安全的影响，提出RMA攻击与防御新范式。

---

- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 多模态语言模型在输入结构（如角色标签、图像位置）被改变时，容易生成有害内容，因模型对这些结构不敏感。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有对齐训练只关注“助手”回复内容，忽略“用户”输入和输入格式变化，导致模型在真实复杂输入下容易被误导。  
  - 真实场景中用户输入结构多样，固定模板无法覆盖，模型需具备结构鲁棒性。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出角色-模态攻击（RMA）：通过调换“用户”和“助手”角色标签，或移动图像token位置，诱导模型生成有害输出。  
  - 攻击不修改内容，仅改变输入结构，更隐蔽且难检测。  
  - 采用对抗训练：在训练中加入多种RMA扰动的有害与正常样本，使模型只关注查询内容，忽略结构干扰。

- 结论是什么？  
  - RMA在8种设置下对多个VLM有效，攻击成功率高，且可组合增强。  
  - 对抗训练显著降低攻击成功率（ASR），同时保持模型正常任务表现，具备实用价值。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次揭示输入结构扰动（角色混淆、图像位置）是MMLM的安全盲点。  
  - 提出RMA攻击与防御一体化新范式，推动模型从“内容对齐”走向“结构鲁棒”对齐。

title: MoEEdit: Efficient and Routing-Stable Knowledge Editing for Mixture-of-Experts LLMs
abstract: Knowledge editing (KE) is crucial for making precise modifications to factual knowledge within large language models (LLMs). Existing KE methods, however, are primarily designed for dense architectures, limiting their applicability to the increasingly popular sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models that power modern scalable LLMs. While MoEs offer remarkable efficiency and capacity scaling, their unique structure introduces new challenges for KE. Naively adapting dense-model editors to MoEs is not only computationally expensive but also induces routing distribution shifts that degrade model stability and consistency. To address these challenges, we introduce MoEEdit, the first systematic framework for routing-stable knowledge editing in MoE LLMs. Our approach reparameterizes expert updates through per-expert null-space projections, ensuring router inputs remain invariant to suppress these shifts, and solves the resulting block-structured optimization with an efficient block coordinate descent (BCD) solver. Experiments demonstrate that MoEEdit achieves state-of-the-art efficacy and generalization, while maintaining high specificity, routing stability, and superior computational and memory efficiency. Our work establishes a robust foundation for scalable and precise knowledge editing in modern sparse LLMs by highlighting the necessity of routing-stable interventions.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 针对稀疏的Mixture-of-Experts（MoE）大模型，实现高效且路由稳定的事实知识编辑。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有知识编辑方法主要针对密集模型，直接用于MoE模型会导致计算开销大、路由分布偏移，影响模型稳定性和一致性。  
  - MoE模型因结构稀疏性成为现代大模型主流，亟需适配的高效编辑方法。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出基于专家级零空间投影的参数重参数化，使专家更新不影响路由输入，从而抑制路由分布变化。  
  - 设计高效的块坐标下降（BCD）求解器，处理块结构优化问题，提升计算效率。  
  - 编辑过程保持路由行为稳定，同时精准修改目标知识。

- 结论是什么？  
  - MoEEdit在编辑效果、泛化能力、特异性上达到SOTA水平。  
  - 显著提升路由稳定性，计算与内存效率优于现有方法。  
  - 适用于大规模稀疏MoE模型的知识精准更新。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首个专为MoE模型设计的路由稳定知识编辑框架。  
  - 引入零空间投影机制，从结构上避免路由扰动，是方法论上的关键突破。  
  - 结合块优化求解策略，兼顾精度与效率，实现可扩展编辑。

title: Model Collapse Is Not a Bug but a Feature in Machine Unlearning for LLMs
abstract: Current unlearning methods for LLMs optimize on the private information they seek to remove by incorporating it into their fine-tuning data. We argue this not only risks reinforcing exposure to sensitive data, it also fundamentally contradicts the principle of minimizing its use. As a remedy, we propose a novel unlearning method—Partial Model Collapse (PMC), which does not require unlearning targets in the unlearning objective. Our approach is inspired by recent observations that training generative models on their own generations leads to distribution collapse, effectively removing information from model outputs. Our central insight is that model collapse can be leveraged for machine unlearning by deliberately triggering it for data we aim to remove. We theoretically analyze that our approach converges to the desired outcome, i.e. the model unlearns the data targeted for removal. We empirically demonstrate that PMC overcomes three key limitations of existing unlearning methods that explicitly optimize on unlearning targets, and more effectively removes private information from model outputs while preserving general model utility. Overall, our contributions represent an important step toward more comprehensive unlearning that aligns with real-world privacy constraints.
explain_zh:
- 问题：现有大模型遗忘方法在训练时反而把要删除的隐私数据“学”得更深，违背了遗忘初衷。
- 动机：避免在遗忘过程中反复使用敏感数据，防止其被模型“固化”。
- 方法：提出“部分模型坍塌”（PMC）方法，不依赖目标数据参与训练，而是通过让模型自我生成并迭代训练，自然“遗忘”特定数据。
- 结论：PMC 能更有效删除隐私信息，同时保持模型整体能力，且不依赖敏感数据参与训练。
- 创新点：首次将“模型坍塌”现象转化为遗忘工具，从“主动删除”转向“自然遗忘”。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 现有大模型遗忘方法在训练中会反复使用要删除的隐私数据，反而加深模型对这些数据的记忆。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 避免在遗忘过程中再次暴露或强化敏感信息，符合隐私保护的“最小使用”原则。  
- 传统方法依赖目标数据参与训练，存在隐私泄露风险。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出“部分模型坍塌”（PMC）方法，不使用待删除数据作为训练目标。  
- 通过让模型基于自身输出进行迭代生成与训练，诱导其内部表示逐渐“坍塌”，从而自然遗忘特定信息。  
- 核心思想：利用模型自我生成导致信息丢失的特性，实现无监督遗忘。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- PMC 能更有效移除隐私信息，且不依赖敏感数据参与训练。  
- 在保持模型通用能力方面优于现有方法。

title: Multi-Feature Quantized Self-Attention for Fair Large Language Models
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) often encode social biases tied to sensitive features such as race and gender, undermining fairness in downstream tasks even after instruction tuning. Conventional debiasing methods require expensive fine-tuning, are tied to specific architectures, or operate only at the input or decoding stage while neglecting attention-level representations, which can result in compromised task performance. Moreover, most approaches are tailored to single-attribute settings and do not explicitly address scenarios with multiple, overlapping protected attributes and their intersections. This paper proposes a novel method of multi-feature quantized attention regularization (MQAR) to mitigate multi-feature bias by injecting a structured quantization into frozen self-attention layers. MQAR disentangles attribute-specific activations through vector-quantized regularization and uses a discriminator-guided autoencoding regularizer to adversarially suppress protected-attribute information while preserving task-relevant semantics. Crucially, the proposed method operates without modifying the backbone parameters or accessing pre-training data, ensuring architecture-agnostic applicability and minimizing representation distortion. MQAR is evaluated on five diverse LLMs (BERT, T5, GPT-Neo, Mixtral, and LLaMA 3.2) using three standard bias benchmarks (WinoBias, StereoSet, and CrowS-Pairs). Across these models, MQAR consistently reduces bias for multiple protected attributes and their intersections while maintaining downstream accuracy within at most 0.4 \%, on average, of non-debiased baselines on sentiment analysis, abusive language detection, and text generation tasks. These findings highlight quantized attention regularization as a scalable and effective method for mitigating social bias in modern language models.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 降低大语言模型中多种社会敏感特征（如性别、种族）及其交叉组合带来的偏见，同时保持模型任务性能。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有去偏方法多需微调、依赖特定架构、仅作用于输入或解码阶段，且难以处理多属性交叉偏见。
  - 模型在注意力层中隐含的偏见会影响公平性，但现有方法未有效干预该层级。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 在冻结的自注意力层中引入结构化量化机制（MQAR），通过向量量化分离敏感特征激活。
  - 使用对抗性自编码器正则项，由判别器引导，主动抑制敏感属性信息。
  - 无需修改模型参数或访问预训练数据，仅在推理时注入正则化。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在5个主流模型（BERT到LLaMA 3.2）上测试，显著降低多属性偏见（WinoBias、StereoSet、CrowS-Pairs）。
  - 任务性能下降极小（平均≤0.4%），适用于文本分类、生成等任务。
  - 方法通用性强，不依赖模型架构或训练数据。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次在注意力层引入量化正则化来解耦敏感特征，实现无参数、无数据的去偏。
  - 显式处理多属性及其交叉偏见，突破单属性限制。
  - 通过对抗性自编码机制，精准抑制偏见信息而不破坏任务语义。

title: No Caption, No Problem: Caption-Free Membership Inference via Model-Fitted Embeddings
abstract: Latent diffusion models have achieved remarkable success in high-fidelity text-to-image generation, but their tendency to memorize training data raises critical privacy and intellectual property concerns. Membership inference attacks (MIAs) provide a principled way to audit such memorization by determining whether a given sample was included in training. However, existing approaches assume access to ground-truth captions. This assumption fails in realistic scenarios where only images are available and their textual annotations remain undisclosed, rendering prior methods ineffective when substituted with vision-language model (VLM) captions. In this work, we propose MoFit , a caption-free MIA framework that constructs synthetic conditioning inputs that are explicitly overfitted to the target model's generative manifold. Given a query image, MoFit proceeds in two stages: (i) model-fitted surrogate optimization, where a perturbation applied to the image is optimized to construct a surrogate in regions of the model’s unconditional prior learned from member samples, and (ii) surrogate-driven embedding extraction, where a model-fitted embedding is derived from the surrogate and then used as a mismatched condition for the query image. This embedding amplifies conditional loss responses for member samples while leaving hold-outs relatively less affected, thereby enhancing separability in the absence of ground-truth captions. Our comprehensive experiments across multiple datasets and diffusion models demonstrate that MoFit consistently outperforms prior VLM-conditioned baselines and achieves performance competitive with caption-dependent methods.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 提出一种无需真实文本描述的会员身份推断方法，用于检测扩散模型是否记忆了特定训练图像。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现实中许多图像缺乏公开文本描述，而现有方法依赖真实标注，无法适用。
  - 扩散模型可能记忆训练数据，需隐私审计工具，但现有工具在无标注场景下失效。
  - 用视觉语言模型生成的描述不准确，导致传统方法性能下降。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 两阶段流程：(i) 通过优化图像扰动，生成一个“代理图像”，使其落在模型对成员样本学习到的无条件先验空间中；(ii) 用该代理图像生成“模型适配嵌入”，作为查询图像的条件输入。
  - 嵌入设计使成员样本产生更大条件损失，增强与非成员样本的区分能力。
  - 整个过程无需真实文本，仅依赖图像和模型本身。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个数据集和扩散模型上，MoFit 性能优于基于 VLM 的现有方法。
  - 接近甚至媲美依赖真实文本的攻击方法。
  - 适用于无文本标注的真实场景，如私有图像库或未公开描述的数据集。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次提出“模型适配嵌入”概念，通过优化代理图像来构建有效条件输入。
  - 不依赖真实或VLM生成的文本，突破了对文本描述的依赖瓶颈。
  - 两阶段设计巧妙结合模型先验与条件损失，实现无文本条件下的高精度攻击。

title: On the Impossibility of Separating Intelligence from Judgment: The Computational Intractability of Filtering for AI Alignment
abstract: With the increased deployment of large language models (LLMs), one concern is their potential misuse for generating harmful content. Our work studies the alignment challenge, with a focus on filters to prevent the generation of unsafe information. Two natural points of intervention are the filtering of the input prompt before it reaches the model, and filtering the output after generation. Our main results demonstrate computational challenges in filtering both prompts and outputs. First, we show that there exist LLMs for which there are no efficient input-prompt filters: adversarial prompts that elicit harmful behavior can be easily constructed, which are computationally indistinguishable from benign prompts for any efficient filter. Our second main result identifies a natural setting in which output filtering is computationally intractable. All of our separation results are under cryptographic hardness assumptions. In addition to these core findings, we also formalize and study relaxed mitigation approaches, demonstrating further computational barriers. We conclude that safety cannot be achieved by designing filters external to the LLM internals (architecture and weights); in particular, black-box access to the LLM will not suffice. Based on our technical results, we argue that an aligned AI system’s intelligence cannot be separated from its judgment.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何有效过滤大语言模型（LLM）的输入和输出，以防止生成有害内容。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 随着LLM广泛应用，其可能被用于生成有害信息，需通过外部过滤机制保障安全。  
  - 现有方法依赖在模型外加“过滤器”来拦截危险输入或输出，但缺乏理论支撑。  
  - 亟需理解这种外部过滤是否在理论上可行。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 从计算复杂性角度建模过滤问题，假设某些数学难题（如密码学难题）无法高效求解。  
  - 构造“对抗性提示”：看似无害但能诱导模型生成有害内容，且无法被高效过滤器识别。  
  - 证明在特定条件下，输出过滤也面临计算不可行性，即使知道模型行为也无法高效判断是否安全。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 外部过滤器无法可靠防止LLM生成有害内容，因对抗性输入/输出在计算上与正常内容无区别。  
  - 安全性不能仅靠“黑盒”过滤实现，必须深入模型内部。  
  - 理智（intelligence）与判断（judgment）不可分离，安全需内嵌于模型本身。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次从计算不可行性角度揭示外部过滤的理论极限，突破传统安全设计思路。  
  - 将密码学假设引入AI对齐研究，提供严谨的理论分析框架。  
  - 提出“智能与判断不可分离”的核心观点，为未来对齐研究指明方向。

title: Optimal Transport-Induced Samples against Out-of-Distribution Overconfidence
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) often produce overconfident predictions on out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs, undermining their reliability in open-world environments. Singularities in semi-discrete optimal transport (OT) mark regions of semantic ambiguity, where classifiers are particularly prone to unwarranted high-confidence predictions. Motivated by this observation, we propose a principled framework to mitigate OOD overconfidence by leveraging the geometry of OT-induced singular boundaries. Specifically, we formulate an OT problem between a continuous base distribution and the latent embeddings of training data, and identify the resulting singular boundaries. By sampling near these boundaries, we construct a class of OOD inputs, termed optimal transport-induced OOD samples (OTIS), which are geometrically grounded and inherently semantically ambiguous. During training, a confidence suppression loss is applied to OTIS to guide the model toward more calibrated predictions in structurally uncertain regions. Extensive experiments show that our method significantly alleviates OOD overconfidence and outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 深度神经网络在分布外（OOD）数据上容易产生过度自信的错误预测，影响其在真实开放环境中的可靠性。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 半离散最优传输（OT）中的奇点区域对应语义模糊地带，模型在此类区域容易做出高置信度但错误的预测。  
  - 通过挖掘这些几何结构，可主动构造具有挑战性的OOD样本，用于训练模型更稳健。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 构建一个连续基分布与训练数据嵌入之间的最优传输问题，识别出OT的奇异边界。  
  - 在这些边界附近采样生成“最优传输诱导的OOD样本”（OTIS），其具有几何合理性与语义模糊性。  
  - 在训练中引入置信度抑制损失，使模型对OTIS样本的预测置信度降低，提升校准性。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 实验表明，该方法显著缓解了OOD场景下的过度自信问题。  
  - 在多个基准上优于当前最先进方法，尤其在高难度OOD检测任务中表现突出。  
  - 适用于图像分类等常见任务，对模型校准性提升具有普适价值。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将最优传输的几何奇点用于生成语义模糊的OOD样本（OTIS）。  
  - 提出基于OT边界采样的主动学习策略，实现对模型不确定性的精准建模。  
  - 将几何结构与模型校准结合，提供一种可解释且有效的OOD鲁棒性增强范式。

title: Optimizing Agent Planning for Security and Autonomy
abstract: Indirect prompt injection attacks threaten AI agents that execute consequential actions, motivating deterministic system-level defenses. Such defenses can provably block unsafe actions by enforcing confidentiality and integrity policies, but currently appear costly: they reduce task completion rates and increase token usage compared to probabilistic defenses. We argue that existing evaluations miss a key benefit of system-level defenses: reduced reliance on human oversight. We introduce autonomy metrics to quantify this benefit: the fraction of consequential actions an agent can execute without human-in-the-loop (HITL) approval while preserving security. To increase autonomy, we design a security-aware agent that (i) introduces richer HITL interactions, and (ii) explicitly plans for both task progress and policy compliance. We implement this agent design atop an existing information-flow control defense against prompt injection and evaluate it on the AgentDojo and WASP benchmarks. Experiments show that this approach yields higher autonomy without sacrificing utility (task completion).

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有系统级防御虽能确保安全，但依赖大量人工审核，降低效率；而当前评估忽略了“减少人工干预”这一关键优势。
  - 真实场景中，人工审核成本高、延迟大，亟需更高效的安全部署方式。

title: Optimizing Canaries for Privacy Auditing with Metagradient Descent
abstract:
In this work we study black-box privacy auditing, where the goal is to lower bound the privacy parameter
of a differentially private learning algorithm using only the algorithm’s outputs (i.e., final trained model).
For DP-SGD (the most successful method for training differentially private deep learning models), the
canonical approach auditing uses membership inference—an auditor comes with a small set of special “ca-
nary” examples, inserts a random subset of them into the training set, and then tries to discern which of
their canaries were included in the training set (typically via a membership inference attack). The audi-
tor’s success rate then provides a lower bound on the privacy parameters of the learning algorithm. Our
main contribution is a method for optimizing the auditor’s canary set to improve privacy auditing, leverag-
ing recent work on metagradient optimization. Our empirical evaluation demonstrates that by
using such optimized canaries, we can improve empirical lower bounds for differentially private image
classification models by over 2x in certain instances. Furthermore, we demonstrate that our method is
transferable and efficient: canaries optimized for non-private SGD with a small model architecture remain
effective when auditing larger models trained with DP-SGD.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何提升黑箱隐私审计中对差分隐私模型的隐私参数下界估计精度。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统“探针”样本（canary）选择随机或启发式，导致隐私审计结果不准确，无法充分暴露模型隐私泄露风险。
  - 现有方法依赖人工设计或固定策略，难以适应复杂模型和数据分布。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出基于元梯度下降（metagradient descent）的优化方法，自动学习最优“探针”样本集。
  - 将探针样本的生成视为可微优化问题，通过元梯度反向传播调整探针特征，使其更易被模型“记住”。
  - 在审计过程中，将优化后的探针插入训练集，通过成员推断攻击判断其是否被训练数据包含。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 实验显示，优化后的探针使隐私下界估计提升超过2倍，显著增强审计能力。
  - 方法具备良好迁移性：小模型上优化的探针可有效用于大模型审计。
  - 适用于图像分类等任务中使用DP-SGD训练的模型。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将元梯度优化引入隐私审计，实现探针样本的自动、可微优化。
  - 无需依赖人工设计或固定规则，提升审计的自动化与有效性。
  - 探针的可迁移性为高效审计提供了新路径，降低计算成本。
