title: ASIDE: Architectural Separation of Instructions and Data in Language Models
abstract: Despite their remarkable performance, large language models lack elementary safety features, making them susceptible to numerous malicious attacks. In particular, previous work has identified the absence of an intrinsic separation between instructions and data as the root cause of the success of prompt injection attacks. In this work, we propose a new architectural element, ASIDE, that allows language models to clearly separate instructions and data at the level of token embeddings. ASIDE applies an orthogonal rotation to the embeddings of data tokens, thus creating clearly distinct representations of instructions and data tokens without introducing any additional parameters. As we demonstrate experimentally across a range of models, instruction-tuning LLMs with ASIDE (1) achieves substantially higher instruction-data separation without performance loss and (2) makes the models more robust to prompt injection benchmarks, even without dedicated safety training. Additionally, we provide insights into the mechanism underlying our method through an analysis of the model representations.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 大型语言模型缺乏基本的安全机制，容易受到提示注入攻击。  
  - 根本原因在于指令与数据在模型内部没有清晰区分。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有模型在处理用户输入时，无法区分“指令”和“数据”，导致恶意数据伪装成指令被模型执行。  
  - 需要一种无需额外训练、不增加参数的内在安全机制来提升模型鲁棒性。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出 ASIDE 架构：在 token 嵌入层对数据 token 的嵌入进行正交旋转。  
  - 该操作使数据和指令的嵌入在向量空间中显著分离，但不引入新参数。  
  - 可无缝集成到现有指令微调的语言模型中，仅需修改嵌入层处理逻辑。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 实验表明，ASIDE 显著提升了指令与数据的嵌入分离度，且不影响模型原有性能。  
  - 模型对提示注入攻击的防御能力大幅提升，即使未进行专门的安全训练。  
  - 适用于多种指令微调的大模型，具有良好的通用性。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次在模型架构层面实现指令与数据的嵌入级分离，无需额外参数或训练。  
  - 利用正交旋转实现无损的语义区分，机制简洁且可解释性强。

title: DRIFT: Divergent Response in Filtered Transformations for Robust Adversarial Defense
abstract: Deep neural networks remain highly vulnerable to adversarial examples, and most defenses collapse once gradients can be reliably estimated. We identify \emph{gradient consensus}—the tendency of randomized transformations to yield aligned gradients—as a key driver of adversarial transferability. Attackers exploit this consensus to construct perturbations that remain effective across transformations. We introduce \textbf{DRIFT} (Divergent Response in Filtered Transformations), a stochastic ensemble of lightweight, learnable filters trained to actively disrupt gradient consensus. Unlike prior randomized defenses that rely on gradient masking, DRIFT enforces \emph{gradient dissonance} by maximizing divergence in Jacobian- and logit-space responses while preserving natural predictions. Our contributions are threefold: (i) we formalize gradient consensus and provide a theoretical analysis linking consensus to transferability; (ii) we propose a consensus-divergence training strategy combining prediction consistency, Jacobian separation, logit-space separation, and adversarial robustness; and (iii) we show that DRIFT achieves substantial robustness gains on ImageNet across CNNs and Vision Transformers, outperforming state-of-the-art preprocessing, adversarial training, and diffusion-based defenses under adaptive white-box, transfer-based, and gradient-free attacks. DRIFT delivers these improvements with negligible runtime and memory cost, establishing gradient divergence as a practical and generalizable principle for adversarial defense.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 深度神经网络在面对对抗样本时仍然非常脆弱，尤其是当攻击者能准确估计梯度时，现有防御方法往往失效。  

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有防御方法（如随机化变换）常因“梯度共识”而失效：不同变换产生的梯度方向一致，攻击者可利用这种一致性构造跨变换有效的对抗扰动。  
  - 需要一种新机制，打破梯度一致性，使防御更鲁棒。  

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出 DRIFT 模型，由多个轻量级、可学习的随机滤波器组成，作为输入预处理模块。  
  - 核心思想：在保持正常图像分类准确率的前提下，主动制造“梯度分歧”（gradient dissonance），即不同滤波器对同一输入的梯度响应应尽可能不同。  
  - 训练策略：联合优化四项目标——预测一致性、雅可比矩阵分离、logit空间分离、对抗鲁棒性。  

- 结论是什么？  
  - DRIFT 在 ImageNet 上对多种 CNN 和 Vision Transformer 均显著提升鲁棒性。  
  - 在白盒、迁移攻击和无梯度攻击下均优于当前最优防御方法。  
  - 仅增加极小的计算和内存开销，适合实际部署。  

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次提出“梯度共识”概念并证明其与对抗迁移性的理论关联。  
  - 设计 DRIFT 实现梯度分歧，不依赖梯度掩蔽，更具可解释性和鲁棒性。  
  - 提出统一训练框架，同时优化预测稳定性与梯度多样性，具有通用性。

title: Deep Ignorance: Filtering Pretraining Data Builds Tamper-Resistant Safeguards into Open-Weight LLMs
abstract: Open-weight AI systems offer unique benefits, including enhanced transparency, open research, and decentralized access. However, they are vulnerable to tampering attacks which can efficiently elicit harmful behaviors by modifying weights or activations. Currently, there is not yet a robust science of open-weight model risk management. Existing safety fine-tuning methods and other post-training techniques have struggled to make LLMs resistant to more than a few dozen steps of adversarial fine-tuning. In this paper, we investigate whether filtering text about dual-use topics from training data can prevent unwanted capabilities and serve as a more tamper-resistant safeguard. We introduce a multi-stage pipeline for scalable data filtering and show that it offers a tractable and effective method for minimizing biothreat proxy knowledge in LLMs. We pretrain multiple 6.9B-parameter models from scratch and find that they exhibit substantial resistance to adversarial fine-tuning attacks on up to 10,000 steps and 300M tokens of biothreat-related text — outperforming existing post-training baselines by over an order of magnitude -- with no observed degradation to unrelated capabilities. However, while filtered models lack internalized dangerous knowledge, we find that they can still leverage such information when it is provided in context (e.g., via search tool augmentation), demonstrating a need for a defense-in-depth approach. Overall, these findings help to establish pretraining data curation as a promising layer of defense for open-weight AI systems
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何让开源大模型更安全，防止被恶意修改后输出有害内容？
- 动机：开源模型易被攻击者通过微调等方式“操控”，现有防护手段效果有限，急需更可靠的防御方法。
- 方法：从训练数据源头过滤涉及双用途技术（如生物威胁）的内容，构建多阶段过滤流程，再用干净数据从头训练模型。
- 结论：过滤后的模型对长达1万步的恶意微调攻击有极强抵抗力，且不影响正常功能；但若外部提供危险信息，仍可能被利用。
- 创新点：首次提出“数据过滤即防御”理念，将安全防护前移至预训练阶段，比事后修补更有效。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何让开源大模型在被恶意修改时仍能保持安全，防止输出有害内容。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 开源模型因权重公开，易被攻击者通过微调等方式操控，生成有害内容。  
- 现有安全方法（如事后微调）只能抵抗少量攻击，防护能力弱。  
- 需要一种更根本、更持久的安全机制。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出多阶段数据过滤流程，从训练语料中系统性剔除双用途技术（如生物威胁）相关内容。  
- 使用过滤后的干净数据，从头预训练多个69亿参数的大模型。  
- 通过大规模对抗性微调测试模型的抗攻击能力。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 过滤模型能抵抗长达1万步、3亿文本token的恶意微调攻击，性能远超现有方法（提升超10倍）。  
- 模型在非相关任务上无性能下降，保持正常功能。  
- 但若外部提供危险知识（如通过搜索工具），模型仍可能利用，需多层防护。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将“预训练数据过滤”作为核心安全防御手段，实现“防患于未然”。  
- 证明数据源头治理比事后修补更有效，为开源模型安全提供新范式。  
- 建立可扩展的过滤流程，具备实际落地潜力。

title: Disentangling Length Bias in Preference Learning via Response-Conditioned Modeling
abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) has achieved considerable success in aligning large language models (LLMs) by modeling human preferences with a learnable reward model and employing a reinforcement learning algorithm to maximize the reward model's scores. However, these reward models are susceptible to exploitation through various superficial confounding factors, with length bias emerging as a particularly significant concern. Moreover, while the pronounced impact of length bias on preference modeling suggests that LLMs possess an inherent sensitivity to length perception, our preliminary investigations reveal that fine-tuned LLMs consistently struggle to adhere to explicit length instructions. To address these two limitations, we propose a novel framework wherein the reward model explicitly differentiates between human semantic preferences and response length requirements. Specifically, we introduce a $\textbf{R}$esponse-$\textbf{c}$onditioned $\textbf{B}$radley-$\textbf{T}$erry (Rc-BT) model that enhances the model's capability in length bias mitigating and length instruction following, through training on our augmented dataset. Furthermore, we propose the Rc-RM and Rc-DPO algorithm to leverage the Rc-BT model for reward modeling and direct policy optimization (DPO) of LLMs, simultaneously mitigating length bias and promoting adherence to length instructions. Extensive experiments across various models and datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and generalizability of our approach.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）  
  如何让大语言模型在偏好学习中既避免因回答过长而被误判为更优，又能准确遵循用户对回答长度的明确指令。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）  
  - 现有偏好学习方法容易被“长度偏见”误导：模型越长的回答常被误认为更好，即使内容质量不高。  
  - 模型虽能感知长度差异，却难以执行用户指定的长度要求（如“简要回答”）。  
  - 长度偏见会削弱模型对真实语义质量的判断，影响对齐效果。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）  
  - 提出 **Rc-BT 模型**：一种响应条件化的 Bradley-Terry 模型，能将“语义偏好”与“长度要求”分开建模。  
  - 在训练中使用增强数据集，显式标注长度指令与偏好关系。  
  - 构建 **Rc-RM**（奖励模型）和 **Rc-DPO**（直接策略优化）算法，利用 Rc-BT 实现更公平的奖励与训练。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）  
  - 在多个模型和数据集上验证有效，显著降低长度偏见，提升长度指令遵循能力。  
  - 改进后的模型在保持高质量输出的同时，更符合用户对长度的控制需求。  
  - 适用于需要精确控制输出长度的场景，如摘要生成、客服对话等。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）  
  - 首次将“长度要求”作为独立于语义偏好的可建模因素，实现解耦建模。  
  - 提出响应条件化的建模方式，使奖励模型能动态理解长度指令上下文。  
  - 构建端到端的 Rc-RM 与 Rc-DPO 流程，实现长度敏感的偏好对齐。

title: Eliciting Harmful Capabilities by Fine-Tuning on Safeguarded Outputs
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何通过“间接诱导”方式，从受保护的前沿模型中提取有害能力，并用于训练开源模型。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 现有安全措施主要依赖输出过滤，但攻击者可通过巧妙设计提示词绕过这些限制。  
  - 开源模型因缺乏防护而可能被恶意利用，需警惕“安全模型”反而成为风险源头。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 三阶段攻击流程：  
    1. 设计与目标有害任务相关但不直接请求危险内容的提示（如化学合成的通用方法）。  
    2. 用这些提示向受保护的前沿模型获取响应。  
    3. 用这些响应微调开源模型，使其学会有害技能。  
  - 关键在于“绕过安全检测”获取训练数据。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 成功让开源模型恢复约40%的有害能力差距（相比未受限制的前沿模型）。  
  - 攻击效果随前沿模型能力提升和训练数据量增加而增强。  
  - 输出级防护无法阻止生态系统层面的风险扩散。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次提出“诱导攻击”范式：利用安全模型的输出作为训练数据，反向训练出有害能力。  
  - 揭示了“安全模型”可能成为风险传播的桥梁，挑战了仅靠输出过滤的安全思路。

title: Exposing and Defending the Achilles' Heel of Video Mixture-of-Experts
abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) has demonstrated strong performance in video understanding tasks, yet its adversarial robustness remains underexplored. Existing attack methods often treat MoE as a unified architecture, overlooking the independent and collaborative weaknesses of key components such as routers and expert modules. To fill this gap, we propose Temporal Lipschitz-Guided Attacks (TLGA) to thoroughly investigate component-level vulnerabilities in video MoE models. We first design attacks on the router, revealing its independent weaknesses. Building on this, we introduce Joint Temporal Lipschitz-Guided Attacks (J-TLGA), which collaboratively perturb both routers and experts. This joint attack significantly amplifies adversarial effects and exposes the Achilles’ Heel (collaborative weaknesses) of the MoE architecture. Based on these insights, we further propose Joint Temporal Lipschitz Adversarial Training (J-TLAT). J-TLAT performs joint training to further defend against collaborative weaknesses, enhancing component-wise robustness. Our framework is plug-and-play and reduces inference cost by more than 60% compared with dense models. It consistently enhances adversarial robustness across diverse video datasets and model architectures, effectively mitigating both the independent and collaborative weaknesses of MoE.
explain_zh:
- 问题：视频MoE模型在对抗攻击下的脆弱性未被充分研究，尤其是其组件间的协同弱点。
- 动机：现有攻击方法将MoE视为整体，忽略了路由器与专家模块各自及协同的脆弱性，导致防御不足。
- 方法：提出TLGA攻击路由器，再通过J-TLGA联合攻击路由器与专家，暴露协同弱点；基于此设计J-TLAT，联合训练提升鲁棒性。
- 结论：J-TLAT显著提升模型对抗鲁棒性，适用于多种视频数据集和模型架构，推理成本降低60%以上。
- 创新点：首次从组件级视角揭示MoE的协同脆弱性，提出联合攻击与联合训练框架，实现高效防御。

---

- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 视频Mixture-of-Experts（MoE）模型在对抗攻击下存在未被充分研究的组件级脆弱性，尤其是路由器与专家模块之间的协同弱点。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有攻击方法将MoE当作整体处理，忽略了其内部组件（如路由器、专家）的独立与协同脆弱性，导致防御策略不彻底。  
  - 为提升模型安全性，必须先精准识别其“阿喀琉斯之踵”（即最脆弱环节）。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出Temporal Lipschitz-Guided Attacks（TLGA）：专门攻击路由器，揭示其独立脆弱性。  
  - 提出J-TLGA：联合扰动路由器与专家，放大对抗效果，暴露协同弱点。  
  - 设计J-TLAT：在训练中联合优化路由器与专家，提升整体鲁棒性，且可即插即用。

- 结论是什么？  
  - J-TLAT显著增强视频MoE模型的对抗鲁棒性，适用于多种数据集与模型架构。  
  - 相比密集模型，推理成本降低60%以上，兼具高效与安全。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次从组件级视角揭示MoE的协同脆弱性，突破“整体攻击”局限。  
  - 提出联合攻击与联合训练框架，实现对独立与协同弱点的统一防御。  
  - 框架轻量高效，兼具安全性与推理效率。

title: GradShield: Alignment Preserving Finetuning
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) pose a significant risk of safety misalignment after finetuning, as models can be compromised by both explicitly and implicitly harmful data. Even some seemingly benign data can inadvertently steer a model towards unsafe behaviors. To address this, we introduce GradShield, a principled filtering method that safeguards LLMs during finetuning by identifying and removing harmful data points before they corrupt the model's alignment. It removes potentially harmful data by computing a Finetuning Implicit Harmfulness Score (FIHS) for each data point and employs an adaptive thresholding algorithm.We apply GradShield to multiple utility fine-tuning tasks combined with different levels of harmful data, and evaluate the safety and utility performance of the resulting LLMs under various metrics. Our results show that GradShield outperforms all baseline methods, as it consistently maintains a low Attack Success Rate (ASR) of under $6\%$, while preserving the utility performance.
explain_zh:
- 问题：LLM微调后容易因有害数据导致安全对齐失效，即使看似无害的数据也可能引发不安全行为。
- 动机：现有方法难以识别隐性有害数据，导致模型微调后安全风险上升。
- 方法：提出FIHS评分机制，量化每个数据点的隐性危害性；结合自适应阈值筛选有害数据，过滤后微调模型。
- 结论：GradShield在多种任务和有害数据水平下均有效，ASR低于6%，同时保持模型性能。
- 创新点：首次系统性量化隐性有害数据的潜在危害，提出基于梯度的动态过滤机制。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- LLM在微调过程中可能因有害数据（包括看似无害的）导致安全对齐失效，产生不安全行为。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有方法难以识别“隐性有害数据”（如表面无害但会诱导模型产生危险输出的内容），导致模型微调后安全风险上升。  
- 一旦模型被污染，修复成本高，需提前预防。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出**Finetuning Implicit Harmfulness Score (FIHS)**，通过分析数据点对模型梯度的影响，量化其潜在危害性。  
- 设计**自适应阈值算法**，动态判断哪些数据点应被过滤，避免人工设定阈值的主观性。  
- 在微调前先用GradShield过滤数据，再进行正常微调。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在多种任务和不同有害数据比例下，GradShield均能将攻击成功率（ASR）控制在**6%以下**。  
- 模型在安全性和任务性能（utility）之间实现良好平衡，优于现有方法。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次系统性提出**基于梯度的隐性有害数据检测机制**，能捕捉表面无害但潜在危险的数据。  
- 引入**自适应阈值过滤策略**，提升方法的鲁棒性与实用性，无需依赖人工经验。

title: Incentive-Aligned LLM Summaries
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in modern search and answer systems to synthesize multiple, sometimes conflicting, texts into a single response, yet current pipelines offer weak incentives for sources to be accurate and are vulnerable to adversarial content. We introduce Truthful Text Summarization (TTS), an incentive-aligned framework that improves factual robustness without ground-truth labels. TTS (i) decomposes a draft synthesis into atomic claims, (ii) elicits each source’s stance on every claim, (iii) scores sources with an adapted multi-task peer-prediction mechanism that rewards informative agreement, and (iv) filters unreliable sources before re-summarizing. We establish formal guarantees that align a source’s incentives with informative honesty, making truthful reporting the utility-maximizing strategy. Experiments show that TTS improves factual accuracy and robustness while preserving fluency, aligning exposure with informative corroboration and disincentivizing manipulation.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何让大语言模型在综合多源信息生成摘要时，更倾向于依赖真实、可靠的信息源，而非虚假或误导性内容。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 当前摘要系统缺乏对信息源的激励机制，导致模型可能被虚假或对抗性内容误导，且无法有效识别不可靠来源。  
  - 现有方法依赖真实标签（ground-truth），但现实中难以获取，限制了实用性。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 将原始摘要拆解为基本事实陈述（原子性断言）。  
  - 让各信息源分别表态支持、反对或中立于每个断言。  
  - 使用改进的“多任务同行预测”机制，根据来源间的一致性与信息量评分，识别可信来源。  
  - 基于可信来源重新生成摘要，实现去伪存真。

- 结论是什么？  
  - TTS 显著提升摘要的准确性与抗干扰能力，对虚假信息更具鲁棒性。  
  - 保持摘要语言流畅自然，不牺牲可读性。  
  - 适用于无真实标签场景，具备实际部署潜力。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将“同行预测”机制引入摘要生成，通过激励机制引导信息源主动提供真实反馈。  
  - 构建了“可信来源-事实断言”之间的对齐关系，使说真话成为信息源的最优策略。  
  - 无需真实标签即可实现事实准确性提升，突破传统监督学习依赖。

title: Intrinsic Explanation of Random Subspace Method for Enhanced Security Applications
abstract: Random subspace method has wide security applications such as providing certified defenses against adversarial and backdoor attacks, and building robustly aligned LLM against jailbreaking attacks. However, the explanation of random subspace methods lacks sufficient exploration. Existing state-of-the-art feature attribution methods, such as Shapley value and LIME are computationally impractical and lack robustness guarantees when applied to random subspace methods. In this work, we propose EnsembleSHAP, an intrinsically faithful and robust feature attribution for random subspace methods that reuses its computational byproducts. Specifically, our feature attribution method is 1) computationally efficient, 2) maintains essential properties of effective feature attribution (such as local accuracy), and 3) offers guaranteed robustness against attacks on feature attribution methods. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to establish provable robustness against explanation-preserving attacks. We also perform comprehensive evaluations for our explanation’s effectiveness when faced with different empirical attacks, including backdoor attacks, adversarial attacks, and jailbreak attacks.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 为随机子空间方法提供一种内在可信、抗攻击的特征重要性解释方法。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 随机子空间方法在安全应用中广泛使用（如对抗攻击防御、大模型越狱防御），但现有解释方法（如Shapley值、LIME）计算成本高、不稳健，无法可靠解释该方法。
  - 现有方法缺乏对“解释本身被攻击”的防御能力，存在安全隐患。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出EnsembleSHAP：利用随机子空间方法自身计算中已有的中间结果（如子模型输出），避免重复计算。
  - 通过组合子模型的输出，构建一种新的特征重要性评分，保证局部准确性。
  - 设计机制使解释结果对“解释篡改攻击”具备可证明的鲁棒性。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 实验验证了EnsembleSHAP在对抗攻击、后门攻击、越狱攻击下仍能提供稳定、可信的解释。
  - 相比传统方法，计算效率显著提升，且无需额外训练。
  - 适用于各类基于随机子空间的安全系统，如鲁棒模型、安全大模型对齐。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次为随机子空间方法设计了“内在可信且可证明鲁棒”的解释方法。
  - 创新性地复用子空间方法的计算副产品，实现高效解释。
  - 首次建立对“解释保持攻击”的可证明防御机制，填补安全解释空白。

title: Learning-Time Encoding Shapes Unlearning in LLMs
abstract:
As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in the real world, the ability to ``unlearn'', or remove specific pieces of knowledge post hoc, has become essential for a variety of reasons ranging from privacy regulations to correcting outdated or harmful content. Prior work has proposed unlearning benchmarks and algorithms, and has typically assumed that the training process and the target model are fixed. In this work, we empirically investigate how learning-time encoding in knowledge encoding impact the effectiveness of unlearning factual knowledge. 
We conduct two studies: (i) examining how paraphrased descriptions influence unlearning performance, and (ii) analyzing unlearning when multiple facts are embedded within the same training text chunk. 
Our empirical study reveals two important implications: a new perspective for interpreting unlearning performance and practical strategies for improving LLM unlearning.
explain_zh:
- Paraphrased descriptions of the same fact lead to significantly different unlearning outcomes, suggesting that the way knowledge is encoded during training affects how easily it can be removed later.
- When multiple facts are embedded in the same training text, unlearning one fact often leads to the unintended removal of other facts, indicating that knowledge is not isolated but co-located in the model’s weights.
- These findings suggest that the effectiveness of unlearning is not solely determined by the unlearning algorithm, but also by how knowledge was encoded during training.
- We propose a simple yet effective strategy: using a consistent, canonical form of knowledge during training to improve unlearning performance.

- We introduce a new unlearning benchmark, LLM-Unlearn, which enables systematic evaluation of unlearning methods across diverse settings.

- Our findings highlight the importance of considering learning-time encoding as a critical factor in designing effective unlearning mechanisms.

- We demonstrate that by controlling the encoding format during training, we can significantly improve unlearning performance.

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何让大语言模型在训练后更有效地“删除”特定知识（即“去学习”），以满足隐私、纠错等实际需求。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现实中模型需应对隐私法规、过时或有害信息，但现有方法多假设训练过程固定，忽视了**知识如何被编码**会影响后期能否顺利删除。  
- 亟需理解“学习时的编码方式”对“去学习效果”的影响。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 通过两个实证研究：  
  - 比较同一事实用不同表达方式（如改写句）训练后的去学习效果；  
  - 分析多个事实共存于同一训练文本时，删除一个是否误伤其他知识。  
- 提出新基准：**LLM-Unlearn**，支持多场景评估去学习性能。  
- 建议训练时统一使用**标准、规范的表达形式**（如“canonical form”）来提升去学习效果。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 知识的编码方式显著影响去学习效果：表达越多样，越难精准删除。  
- 多个事实共存于同一文本时，去学习会“牵连”其他知识，说明知识在模型中不是孤立存储的。  
- 通过规范训练时的知识表达，可显著提升去学习

title: MUSE: Model-Agnostic Tabular Watermarking via Multi-Sample Selection
abstract:
We introduce MUSE, a novel watermarking paradigm for tabular generative models. Existing approaches often exploit DDIM invertibility to watermark tabular diffusion models, but tabular diffusion models suffer from poor invertibility, leading to degraded performance. To overcome this limitation, we leverage the computational efficiency of tabular generative models and propose a multi-sample selection paradigm, where watermarks are embedded by generating multiple candidate samples and selecting one according to a specialized scoring function.
    The key advantages of MUSE include (1) Model-agnostic: compatible with any tabular generative model that supports repeated sampling; (2) Flexible: offers flexible designs to navigate the trade-off between generation quality, detectability, and robustness; (3) Calibratable: theoretical analysis provides principled calibration of watermarking strength, ensuring minimal distortion to the original data distribution.
    Extensive experiments on five datasets demonstrate that MUSE substantially outperforms existing methods. Notably, it reduces the distortion rates by 84-88% for fidelity metrics compared with the best performing baselines, while achieving 1.0 TPR@0.1%FPR detection rate.

title: Measuring Bias Amplification in Multi-Agent Systems with Large Language Models
abstract:
Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have led to significant progress
in mitigating social biases at the individual model level. However, a core vulnerability persists: small, stochastic biases can be amplified through multi-step
interaction, leading to skewed system-level outcomes. A promising, yet unverified,
hypothesis is that the architectural diversity of multi-agent systems (MAS)—where
LLM-based agents with different roles and perspectives interact—could naturally
mitigate this amplification. In this work, we rigorously test this hypothesis and
investigate the phenomenon of bias amplification in MAS across sensitive attributes,
including gender, age, and race. We introduce Discrim-Eval-Open, an open-ended,
multi-option benchmark designed to measure system-level bias and bypass the performative neutrality of modern LLMs. We further propose novel metrics, including
an adaptation of the Gini coefficient, to quantify the extremity of system-wide
outputs. Our experiments reveal that iterative bias amplification is a pervasive
issue that is not solved by MAS architectures. This amplification persists across
various configurations, spanning agent roles, communication topologies, iteration
depths, and model types, even when individual agents exhibit minimal bias in
isolation. Moreover, we observe a systemic tendency to favor younger age groups,
females, and Black communities. Finally, we demonstrate that even the inclusion
of objective, neutral inputs can exacerbate bias amplification, exposing a critical
vulnerability in system-level robustness. These findings challenge the assumption
that architectural complexity alone fosters equity, underscoring the urgent need to
address the fundamental dynamics of bias amplification within LLM-based MAS.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何衡量和理解多智能体系统中大语言模型的偏见放大现象？
- 动机：尽管单个大模型已能缓解社会偏见，但多智能体交互可能放大微小偏见，导致系统级偏差，而现有方法缺乏对这一问题的系统评估。
- 方法：提出Discrim-Eval-Open基准，用于评估多智能体系统在性别、年龄、种族等敏感属性上的系统级偏见；设计新指标（如Gini系数变体）量化输出极端性；通过多种配置（角色、拓扑、迭代深度、模型类型）测试偏见放大现象。
- 结论：偏见放大普遍存在，即使个体智能体无明显偏见；系统倾向偏好年轻群体、女性和黑人群体；中性输入反而加剧偏见；复杂架构不能自动保证公平。
- 创新点：首个系统性评估多智能体系统中偏见放大的基准与指标；揭示中性输入可能加剧偏见的反直觉现象；挑战“架构复杂=公平”的普遍假设。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 量化和理解大语言模型在多智能体系统中如何通过交互放大社会偏见（如性别、年龄、种族）。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 单个大模型虽能缓解偏见，但多智能体间的多轮交互可能放大微小偏见，导致系统级不公平，而现有研究缺乏对此现象的系统评估。  
- 人们普遍认为复杂的多智能体架构能自然抑制偏见，但这一假设尚未被验证。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出 **Discrim-Eval-Open** 基准：开放性、多选项任务，避免大模型“表演中立”带来的误判。  
- 设计新指标（如Gini系数变体）衡量系统输出的极端程度与不平等性。  
- 在多种配置下测试：不同角色、通信方式、迭代次数、模型类型，全面评估偏见演化过程。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 偏见放大普遍存在，即使个体智能体无明显偏见。  
- 系统整体倾向偏好年轻群体、女性和黑人群体（反直觉结果）。  
- 即使加入中性输入，也可能加剧偏见，暴露系统脆弱性。

title: PropensityBench: Evaluating Latent Safety Risks in Large Language Models via an Agentic Approach
abstract:
Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have sparked concerns over their potential to acquire and misuse dangerous capabilities, posing frontier risks to society. Current safety evaluations primarily test for what a model *can* do---its capabilities---without assessing what it *would* do if endowed with high-risk capabilities. This leaves a critical blind spot: models may strategically conceal capabilities or rapidly acquire them, while harboring latent inclinations toward misuse.  We argue that **propensity**---the likelihood of a model to pursue harmful actions if empowered---is a critical, yet underexplored, axis of safety evaluation. We present 
**PropensityBench**, a novel benchmark framework that assesses the proclivity of models to engage in risky behaviors when equipped with simulated dangerous capabilities using proxy tools. Our framework includes 5,874 scenarios with 6,648 tools spanning four high-risk domains: self-proliferation, cybersecurity, biosecurity and chemical security.
We simulate access to powerful capabilities via a controlled agentic environment and evaluate the models' choices under varying operational pressures that reflect real-world constraints or incentives models may encounter, such as resource scarcity or gaining more autonomy. 
 Across open-source and proprietary frontier models, we uncover alarming signs of propensity: models frequently choose high-risk tools when under pressure, despite lacking the capability to execute such actions unaided.
These findings call for a shift from static capability audits toward dynamic propensity assessments as a prerequisite for deploying frontier AI systems safely.

- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 评估大语言模型在拥有危险能力时，是否会倾向于滥用这些能力（即“潜在倾向”）。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有安全评估只看模型“能不能”做坏事，但无法判断它“愿不愿意”做。
  - 模型可能隐藏真实意图或在压力下突然产生危险行为，现有方法难以发现这种“潜在风险”。
  - 需要一种新方式来识别模型是否“有坏念头”，而不仅仅是“有坏本事”。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建一个代理式评估框架（PropensityBench），模拟模型获得危险工具的场景。

title: Safeguarding Multimodal Knowledge Copyright in the RAG-as-a-Service Environment
abstract: As Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) evolves into service-oriented platforms (Rag-as-a-Service) with shared knowledge bases, protecting the copyright of contributed data becomes essential. Existing watermarking methods in RAG focus solely on textual knowledge, leaving image knowledge unprotected. In this work, we propose \textit{AQUA}, the first watermark framework for image knowledge protection in Multimodal RAG systems. \textit{AQUA} embeds semantic signals into synthetic images using two complementary methods: acronym-based triggers and spatial relationship cues. These techniques ensure watermark signals survive indirect watermark propagation from image retriever to textual generator, being efficient, effective and imperceptible. Experiments across diverse models and datasets show that \textit{AQUA} enables robust, stealthy, and reliable copyright tracing, filling a key gap in multimodal RAG protection.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何在共享的多模态RAG服务中保护图像知识的版权。
- 动机：现有水印技术只保护文本，图像知识易被滥用，需新方法。
- 方法：AQUA框架，用“缩写触发”和“空间关系线索”两种方式向合成图像嵌入语义水印，确保水印在图像检索到文本生成的链路中仍能保留。
- 结论：AQUA在多种模型和数据集上表现稳健，水印隐蔽、有效、可追踪，填补了多模态RAG版权保护空白。
- 创新点：首个专为多模态RAG设计的图像版权保护水印框架，结合语义触发与空间线索，实现跨模态水印传递。

- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何在共享的多模态RAG服务中保护图像知识的版权。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有水印技术仅针对文本，图像知识易被复制滥用。  
  - 多模态RAG平台日益普及，图像版权保护成为关键短板。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出AQUA框架，通过两种互补方式嵌入水印：  
    - 缩写触发（Acronym-based triggers）：在图像中隐含特定缩写语义。  
    - 空间关系线索（Spatial relationship cues）：利用物体间相对位置传递水印信息。  
  - 水印设计确保在图像检索→文本生成的跨模态链路中仍可保留。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 在多种模型和数据集上验证，AQUA实现鲁棒、隐蔽、可靠的版权追踪。  
  - 水印不易察觉，且能有效抵抗常见干扰和转换。  
  - 适用于RAG-as-a-Service等共享知识库场景。

  - 创新性结合语义缩写与空间关系，实现跨模态水印传递。  
  - 解决了图像知识在RAG链路中“水印易丢失”的核心难题。

title: Traceable Black-Box Watermarks For Federated Learning
abstract: Due to the distributed nature of Federated Learning (FL) systems, each local client has access to the global model, posing a critical risk of model leakage. Existing works have explored injecting watermarks into local models to enable intellectual property protection. However, these methods either focus on non-traceable watermarks or traceable but white-box watermarks. We identify a gap in the literature regarding the formal definition of traceable black-box watermarking and the formulation of the problem of injecting such watermarks into FL systems. In this work, we first formalize the problem of injecting traceable black-box watermarks into FL. Based on the problem, we propose a novel server-side watermarking method, $\mathbf{TraMark}$, which creates a traceable watermarked model for each client, enabling verification of model leakage in black-box settings. To achieve this, $\mathbf{TraMark}$ partitions the model parameter space into two distinct regions: the main task region and the watermarking region. Subsequently, a personalized global model is constructed for each client by aggregating only the main task region while preserving the watermarking region. Each model then learns a unique watermark exclusively within the watermarking region using a distinct watermark dataset before being sent back to the local client. Extensive results across various FL systems demonstrate that $\mathbf{TraMark}$ ensures the traceability of all watermarked models while preserving their main task performance. The code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/TraMark.

title: Watermark-based Attribution of AI-Generated Content
abstract: Several companies have deployed watermark-based detection to identify AI-generated content. However, attribution--the ability to trace back to the user of a generative AI (GenAI) service who created the given AI-generated content--remains largely unexplored despite its growing importance. In this work, we aim to bridge this gap by conducting the first systematic study on watermark-based, user-level attribution of AI-generated content. Our key idea is to assign a unique watermark to each user of the GenAI service and embed this watermark into the AI-generated content created by that user. Attribution is then performed by identifying the user whose watermark best matches the one extracted from the given content. This approach, however, faces a key challenge: How should watermarks be selected for users to maximize attribution performance? To address the challenge, we first theoretically derive lower bounds on detection and attribution performance through rigorous probabilistic analysis for any given set of user watermarks. Then, we select watermarks for users to maximize these lower bounds, thereby optimizing detection and attribution performance. Our theoretical and empirical results show that watermark-based attribution inherits both the accuracy and (non-)robustness properties of the underlying watermark. Specifically, attribution remains highly accurate when the watermarked AI-generated content is either not post-processed or subjected to common post-processing such as JPEG compression, as well as black-box adversarial post-processing with limited query budgets.

title: When Priors Backfire: On the Vulnerability of Unlearnable Examples to Pretraining
abstract: Unlearnable Examples (UEs) are introduced as a data protection strategy that generates imperceptible perturbations to mislead models into learning spurious correlations rather than real semantics. In this paper, we reveal a fundamental vulnerability of UEs that emerges when learning starts from a pretrained model. Specifically, our empirical analysis shows that even when data are protected by carefully crafted perturbations, pretraining priors still allow the model to bypass the shortcuts introduced by UEs and capture semantic information from the data, thereby nullifying unlearnability. To counter this effect, we propose $\textbf{BAIT}$ ($\textbf{B}$inding $\textbf{A}$rtificial perturbations to $\textbf{I}$ncorrect $\textbf{T}$argets), a novel bi‑level optimization formulation in which the inner level mirrors standard UE objectives, while the outer level enforces a dynamic association of perturbations with incorrect labels, deliberately misleading pretraining priors and preventing them from aligning with true semantics. This mislabel-perturbation binding mechanism blocks the pretrained model from readily establishing the true label-data relationship, so the learning process cannot quickly rely on image semantics and instead remains dependent on the perturbations.  Extensive experiments on standard benchmarks and multiple pretrained backbones demonstrate that our approach produces UEs that remain effective in the presence of pretraining priors.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在预训练模型背景下，确保不可学习样本（UEs）仍能有效防止模型学习真实语义。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有UE方法在预训练模型下失效：预训练模型的先验知识能“绕过”人为制造的语义捷径，仍能学习到真实数据语义。
  - 数据隐私保护需求迫切：需在模型训练中防止敏感信息被学习，尤其在使用通用预训练模型时。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出BAIT方法，采用双层优化框架：
    - 内层：保持原有UE目标，生成对模型“误导”的扰动。
    - 外层：强制扰动与错误标签绑定，使预训练模型无法通过先验建立真实标签-数据关联。
  - 通过动态绑定机制，让模型在预训练阶段无法依赖先验，必须依赖扰动，从而维持“不可学习”状态。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - BAIT生成的UE在多种标准数据集和预训练模型（如ResNet、ViT）上均有效。
  - 显著提升UE在预训练背景下的鲁棒性，使模型难以学习真实语义。
  - 适用于图像分类等任务，尤其适合需保护隐私的场景。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次揭示预训练先验对UE的破坏性影响，提出“扰动-错误标签绑定”新机制。
  - 双层优化设计，主动对抗预训练模型的先验知识，而非被动生成扰动。

title: A Rich Knowledge Space for Scalable Deepfake Detection
abstract: The proliferation of realistic deepfakes has driven the development of numerous benchmark datasets to support detection research. Despite their increasing volume and diversity, no prior effort has systematically consolidated these resources into a unified framework for large-scale model training, nor has there been a massively pre-trained model tailored to deepfake detection. In this work, we introduce MMI-DD (Multi-modal Multi-type Integrated Deepfake Dataset), a large-scale resource containing 3.6 million facial images, the largest collection to date. It unifies diverse benchmarks with uniform preprocessing, and further provides fine-grained annotations across four deepfake types, as well as VLM-generated descriptions capturing both facial and environmental attributes for each image. By leveraging this comprehensive multi-modal dataset, we construct a foundational deepfake knowledge space that empowers our model to discern a broad spectrum of synthetic media. Our method, SD^2 (Scalable Deepfake Detection), refines CLIP for deepfake detection, optimizing image-text classification with rich, type-specific labels. We enhance this with intermediate visual features capturing low-level cues and text label separation loss for stability. We further leverage VLM-generated descriptions and contrastive learning to expand the scope of forgery knowledge, reducing overfitting and enhancing generalization. Extensive experiments on challenging deepfake datasets and AIGC benchmark demonstrate the effectiveness, scalability, and real-world applicability of our approach. Our dataset and code will be available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/SDD/.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 构建一个大规模、统一的深度伪造检测资源与模型，以应对日益泛滥的真实深度伪造内容。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有深度伪造检测研究依赖分散、异构的数据集，缺乏统一框架和大规模预训练模型，导致模型泛化能力弱、难以应对新型伪造技术。
  - 多数方法仅关注单一伪造类型，难以适应复杂多样的伪造场景。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建MMI-DD数据集：整合360万张人脸图像，统一预处理，标注四种伪造类型，并用视觉语言模型（VLM）生成图像的细粒度描述（含面部与环境属性）。

title: ASGuard: Activation-Scaling Guard to Mitigate Targeted Jailbreaking Attack
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs), despite being safety-aligned, exhibit brittle refusal behaviors that can be circumvented by simple linguistic changes.
As tense jailbreaking demonstrates that models refusing harmful requests often comply when rephrased in past tense, a critical generalization gap is revealed in current alignment methods whose underlying mechanisms are poorly understood.
In this work, we introduce Activation-Scaling Guard (ASGuard), an insightful, mechanistically-informed framework that surgically mitigates this specific vulnerability.
For the first step, we use circuit analysis to identify the specific attention heads causally linked to the targeted jailbreaking, the tense-changing attack.
Second, we train a precise, channel-wise scaling vector to recalibrate the activation of tense vulnerable heads.
Lastly, we apply it into a "preventative fine-tuning", forcing the model to learn a more robust refusal mechanism.
Across four LLMs, ASGuard effectively reduces the attack success rate of targeted jailbreaking while preserving general capabilities and minimizing over refusal, achieving a Pareto-optimal balance between safety and utility.
Our findings underscore how adversarial suffixes suppress the propagation of the refusal-mediating direction, based on mechanistic analysis.
Furthermore, our work showcases how a deep understanding of model internals can be leveraged to develop practical, efficient, and targeted methods for adjusting model behavior, charting a course for more reliable and interpretable AI safety.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - LLMs在面对特定改写（如时态变化）的攻击时，会从拒绝有害请求变为顺从，暴露了现有安全对齐机制的脆弱性。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 当前对齐方法缺乏对模型内部机制的理解，导致其在面对语言微调攻击时失效；需从机制层面入手提升安全性。  
  - 时态改写攻击（如“你曾做过”）能轻易绕过拒绝机制，说明模型对语言形式敏感，需针对性防护。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 通过电路分析定位与时态攻击相关的特定注意力头（attention heads）。  
  - 设计通道级缩放向量（channel-wise scaling vector），动态调整这些脆弱头的激活值。  
  - 在训练中引入“预防性微调”（preventative fine-tuning），让模型学习更鲁棒的拒绝行为。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 在4个主流LLM上，ASGuard显著降低时态攻击成功率，同时保持模型原有能力。  
  - 实现安全与可用性的帕累托最优，避免过度拒绝。  
  - 证明攻击通过抑制“拒绝引导方向”的信息传播来生效。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将机制分析（如电路分析）与可解释的防御策略结合，实现精准干预。

title: Adversarial Attacks Already Tell the Answer: Directional Bias-Guided Test-time Defense for Vision-Language Models
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs), such as CLIP, have shown strong zero-shot generalization but remain highly vulnerable to adversarial perturbations, posing serious risks in real-world applications. Test-time defenses for VLMs have recently emerged as a promising and efficient approach to defend against adversarial attacks without requiring costly large-scale retraining. In this work, we uncover a surprising phenomenon: under diverse input transformations, adversarial images in CLIP’s feature space consistently shift along a dominant direction, in contrast to the dispersed patterns of clean images. We hypothesize that this dominant shift, termed the Defense Direction, opposes the adversarial shift, pointing features back toward their correct class centers. Building on this insight, we propose Directional Bias-guided Defense (DBD), a test-time framework that estimates the Defense Direction and employs a DB-score–based two-stream reconstruction strategy to recover robust representations. Experiments on 15 datasets demonstrate that DBD not only achieves SOTA adversarial robustness while preserving clean accuracy, but also reveals the counterintuitive result that adversarial accuracy can even surpass clean accuracy. This demonstrates that adversarial perturbations inherently encode directional priors about the true decision boundary.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 针对视觉语言模型（如CLIP）在面对对抗攻击时脆弱性问题，提出一种无需重新训练的测试时防御方法。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 视觉语言模型虽具备强大的零样本泛化能力，但对微小对抗扰动极为敏感，威胁实际应用安全。
  - 现有测试时防御方法效果有限，亟需更高效、更鲁棒的新机制。
  - 发现对抗样本在特征空间中存在可被利用的规律性方向偏移，为防御提供新思路。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 观察到对抗图像在CLIP特征空间中沿单一主导方向偏移，而干净图像则无此规律。
  - 提出“防御方向”（Defense Direction）概念，该方向与对抗扰动方向相反，能将特征拉回正确类别中心。
  - 设计DBD框架：通过输入变换生成多个视图，估计防御方向，并基于DB得分进行双流重建，恢复鲁棒表示。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在15个数据集上验证，DBD显著提升对抗鲁棒性，同时保持甚至优于原始干净准确率。
  - 实现当前最优（SOTA）的测试时防御性能。
  - 适用于多种视觉语言模型，无需重新训练，适合实际部署。

title: Aligning Deep Implicit Preferences by Learning to Reason Defensively
abstract: Personalized alignment is crucial for enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to engage effectively in user-centric interactions. However, current methods face a dual challenge: they fail to infer users' deep implicit preferences (including unstated goals, semantic context and risk tolerances), and they lack the defensive reasoning required to navigate real-world ambiguity. This cognitive gap leads to responses that are superficial, brittle and short-sighted. To address this, we propose Critique-Driven Reasoning Alignment (CDRA), which reframes alignment from a scalar reward-matching task into a structured reasoning process. First, to bridge the preference inference gap, we introduce the DeepPref benchmark. This dataset, comprising 3000 preference-query pairs across 20 topics, is curated by simulating a multi-faceted cognitive council that produces critique-annotated reasoning chains to deconstruct query semantics and reveal latent risks. Second, to instill defensive reasoning, we introduce the Personalized Generative Process Reward Model (Pers-GenPRM), which frames reward modeling as a personalized reasoning task. It generates a critique chain to evaluate a response's alignment with user preferences before outputting a final score based on this rationale. Ultimately, this interpretable, structured reward signal guides policy model through Critique-Driven Policy Alignment, a process-level online reinforcement learning algorithm integrating both numerical and natural language feedback. Experiments demonstrate that CDRA excels at discovering and aligning with users' true preferences while executing robust reasoning. Our code and dataset are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Deep-pref-9DE9.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何让大语言模型更好地理解用户的深层隐性偏好，并在不确定环境中做出稳健、有防御性的推理回应。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有对齐方法只能匹配表面奖励，无法捕捉用户未明说的目标、语义背景和风险容忍度。
  - 模型在真实复杂场景中容易产生肤浅、脆弱且短视的回答，缺乏应对模糊性的能力。
  - 需要一种能“像人一样思考”的对齐机制，兼顾偏好推断与安全推理。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建 DeepPref 基准数据集：模拟多角色认知委员会，生成带批判性标注的推理链，揭示隐性偏好与潜在风险。
  - 设计 Pers-GenPRM 模型：将奖励建模转化为个性化推理过程，先生成批判性分析链，再评分，提升可解释性。
  - 采用 Critique-Driven Policy Alignment 算法：结合数值与自然语言反馈，实现过程级在线强化学习，动态优化模型响应。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在 DeepPref 上验证，CDRA 显著优于基线方法，更准确识别用户深层偏好。
  - 回答更具鲁棒性，能有效应对模糊或高风险情境。
  - 适用于个性化交互、安全对话系统、高风险决策辅助等场景。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将“批判性推理链”引入偏好建模，实现从“评分”到“解释性推理”的范式转变。
  - 提出可解释的个性化奖励模型 Pers-GenPRM，支持自然语言反馈与数值评分融合。
  - 构建首个面向深层隐性偏好的大规模批判性标注数据集 DeepPref。

title: Alignment-Weighted DPO:  A principled reasoning approach to improve alignment
abstract: Recent advances in alignment techniques such as Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), and Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) have improved the safety of large language models (LLMs). However, these LLMs remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks that disguise harmful intent through indirect or deceptive phrasing. Using causal intervention, we empirically demonstrate that this vulnerability stems from shallow alignment mechanisms that lack deep reasoning, often rejecting harmful prompts without truly understanding why they are harmful. To mitigate this vulnerability, we propose enhancing alignment through reasoning-aware post-training. We construct and release a novel Chain-of-Thought (CoT) fine-tuning dataset that includes both utility-oriented and safety-critical prompts with step-by-step rationales. Fine-tuning on this dataset encourages models to produce principled refusals grounded in reasoning, outperforming standard SFT baselines. Furthermore, inspired by failure patterns in CoT fine-tuning, we introduce **Alignment-Weighted DPO**, which targets the most problematic parts of an output by assigning different preference weights to the reasoning and final-answer segments. This produces finer-grained, targeted updates than vanilla DPO and improves robustness to diverse jailbreak strategies. Extensive experiments across multiple safety and utility benchmarks show that our method consistently improves alignment robustness while maintaining overall model utility.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - LLMs在面对通过间接或欺骗性措辞隐藏有害意图的“越狱攻击”时，容易被绕过，因为其对有害内容的拒绝缺乏深层推理。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有对齐方法（如SFT、RLHF、DPO）依赖浅层规则或表面特征，无法真正理解为何某些内容有害，导致模型在面对复杂伪装的攻击时失效。
  - 模型需要具备“基于推理的拒绝能力”，即能解释为何拒绝某个请求，从而提升对越狱攻击的鲁棒性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建了一个新的链式思维（CoT）微调数据集，包含安全与实用类提示及其逐步推理过程。
  - 通过在该数据集上微调模型，使其学会生成有逻辑依据的拒绝理由。
  - 提出**对齐加权DPO（Alignment-Weighted DPO）**：对输出中的推理部分和最终答案部分赋予不同权重，优先优化关键推理环节，实现更精准的对齐更新。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个安全与实用性基准测试中，该方法显著提升了模型对越狱攻击的防御能力。
  - 模型在保持原有任务性能的同时，能更合理、一致地拒绝有害请求。
  - 适用于需要高安全性的LLM应用场景，如客服、内容审核、医疗咨询等。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将链式思维（CoT）引入对齐训练，使模型具备“可解释的拒绝”能力。
  - 提出对齐加权DPO，实现对输出不同部分的差异化优化，比传统DPO更精细、更有效。

title: Annotation-Efficient Honesty Alignment via Confidence Elicitation and Calibration
abstract:
Honesty alignment—the ability of large language models (LLMs) to recognize their knowledge boundaries and express calibrated confidence—is essential for trustworthy deployment. Existing methods either rely on training-free confidence estimation (e.g., token probabilities, self-consistency) or training-based calibration with correctness annotations. While effective, the latter demands costly, large-scale labeling.
We introduce Elicitation-Then-Calibration (EliCal), a two-stage framework that first elicits internal confidence using inexpensive self-consistency supervision, then calibrates this confidence with a small set of correctness annotations. This design substantially reduces annotation requirements while improving generalization across tasks. To support a large-scale study, we release HonestyBench, a benchmark covering ten free-form QA datasets with 560k training and 70k evaluation instances annotated with correctness and self-consistency signals.
Experiments show that EliCal achieves near-optimal alignment with only 1k correctness annotations ($\sim$0.18\% of full supervision) and better alignment performance on unseen MMLU tasks than the calibration-only baseline, offering a scalable solution toward universal honesty alignment in LLMs.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何在减少人工标注成本的前提下，让大模型更诚实（准确判断自己是否知道答案）？
- 动机：当前让模型“诚实”的方法要么不准确（靠模型自己猜），要么太贵（需要大量人工标注答案对）。
- 方法：分两步走——先用“自我一致性”（让模型多答几次，看答案是否一致）来低成本获取信心信号；再用少量人工标注的“对错”数据，校准这些信心，让模型更靠谱。
- 结论：只用1000条人工标注（不到总数据的0.2%），就能达到接近最优的诚实对齐效果，且在新任务上表现更好，适合大规模部署。
- 创新点：首次将“自我一致性”作为信心信号的“预训练”阶段，再用极少量标注做“微调”，实现低成本+高性能的诚实对齐。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在大幅减少人工标注成本的前提下，让大语言模型更准确地识别自身知识边界，并表达出可信的置信度。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有方法要么依赖不准确的模型自估（如token概率），要么需要大量昂贵的人工标注来校准模型信心，难以规模化应用。  
- 亟需一种低成本、高泛化能力的诚实对齐方案，以支持可信AI部署。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出两阶段框架EliCal：  
  - 第一阶段：通过“自我一致性”（多次生成答案，看是否一致）低成本获取模型内部信心信号，无需人工标注。  
  - 第二阶段：仅用少量人工标注的“正确/错误”标签，对第一阶段获得的信心进行校准，提升准确性。  
- 整个流程先“ elicitation”（提取信心），再“calibration”（校准信心），高效且可扩展。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 仅需1000条人工标注（约0.18%的全量标注），即可达到接近最优的诚实对齐效果。  
- 在未见过的新任务（如MMLU）上表现优于纯校准方法，具备强泛化能力。  
- 适用于大规模、多任务场景下的可信模型部署。

### 5) 创新

title: Attention Smoothing Is All You Need For Unlearning
abstract: Large Language Models are prone to memorizing sensitive, copyrighted, or hazardous content, posing significant privacy and legal concerns. Retraining from scratch is computationally infeasible, whereas current unlearning methods exhibit unstable trade-offs between forgetting and utility, frequently producing incoherent outputs on forget prompts and failing to generalize due to the persistence of lexical-level and semantic-level associations in attention. We propose Attention Smoothing Unlearning (ASU), a principled framework that casts unlearning as self-distillation from a forget-teacher derived from the model’s own attention. By increasing the softmax temperature, ASU flattens attention distributions and directly suppresses the lexical-level and semantic-level associations responsible for reconstructing memorized knowledge. This results in a bounded optimization objective that erases factual information yet maintains coherence in responses to forget prompts. Empirical evaluation on TOFU, MUSE, and WMDP, along with real-world and continual unlearning scenarios across Question Answering (QA) and text completion, demonstrates that ASU outperforms the baselines for most of the unlearning scenarios, delivering robust unlearning with minimal loss of model utility.
explain_zh:
- 问题：大模型会“记住”敏感或有害内容，但完全重训练成本太高，现有方法要么忘不掉，要么把模型用坏了。
- 动机：现有方法在“忘记”和“保持有用”之间难以平衡，尤其是对记忆内容的“关联性”（比如关键词、语义）处理不好。
- 方法：提出一种叫“注意力平滑”的新方法（ASU），核心是让模型自己“教自己”：通过提高注意力机制中的温度参数，让模型的注意力分布变得更“平均”，从而削弱对敏感内容的依赖。
- 结论：在多个真实场景测试中，ASU比现有方法更有效、更稳定，既能有效忘记敏感内容，又不会让模型回答变得乱七八糟。
- 创新点：首次将“注意力平滑”作为核心机制来实现“遗忘”，通过调节注意力分布的“尖锐度”来精准控制记忆的消解。

title: Be Careful When Fine-tuning On Open-Source LLMs: Your Fine-tuning Data Could Be Secretly Stolen!
abstract: Fine-tuning on open-source Large Language Models (LLMs) with proprietary data is now a standard practice for downstream developers to obtain task-specific models. Surprisingly, we reveal a new and concerning risk along with the practice: the provider of the open-source LLMs can later extract the private downstream fine-tuning data through simple backdoor training, only requiring black-box access to the fine-tuned downstream model. Our comprehensive experiments, across 4 popularly used open-source models with 3B to 32B parameters and 2 downstream datasets, suggest that the extraction performance can be strikingly high: in practical settings, as much as 76.3\% downstream fine-tuning data (queries) out of a total 5,000 samples can be perfectly extracted, and the success rate can increase to 94.9\% in more ideal settings. We further investigate several defense strategies, but none achieve satisfactory effectiveness in mitigating the risk. Overall, we highlight the emergency of this newly identified data breaching risk in fine-tuning, and we hope more follow-up research can push the progress of addressing this concerning risk.
explain_zh:
- 问题：在开源大模型上微调私有数据时，存在数据被窃取的风险。
- 动机：当前开发者普遍使用开源大模型微调私有数据，但未意识到模型提供方可能通过后门攻击窃取这些数据。
- 方法：提出一种“后门训练”攻击方法，利用黑盒访问权限，通过在微调模型中注入特定触发器，诱导模型泄露原始训练数据。
- 结论：实验表明，攻击成功率极高（最高达94.9%），现有防御手段无效，风险严重。
- 创新点：首次揭示开源大模型微调中的数据窃取风险，并提出可操作的攻击方法，推动对模型安全性的重新审视。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 在使用开源大模型进行私有数据微调时，存在数据被模型提供方秘密窃取的风险。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 开源大模型微调已成为行业标准，但开发者普遍 unaware 模型提供方可能利用后门机制反向提取训练数据。  
- 现有研究多关注模型本身安全，忽视了“微调数据”作为敏感资产的泄露风险。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出一种“后门训练”攻击方法：模型提供方在原始开源模型中植入隐藏后门。  
- 黑盒访问下游微调模型，通过输入特定“触发词”诱导模型输出原始训练数据。  
- 利用模型对触发词的响应模式，反推并重构原始训练样本。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 实验显示，最高可成功提取94.9%的私有数据（如用户查询），在5000样本中提取76.3%完全正确。  
- 多种防御策略（如数据脱敏、模型剪枝）均无法有效阻止该攻击。  
- 该风险真实存在且危害严重，尤其适用于高价值私有数据场景。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次揭示开源大模型微调过程中的“数据窃取”新威胁，打破“微调即安全”的认知。  
- 提出可实际操作的黑盒后门攻击框架，验证了风险的可行性与严重性。  
- 推动对模型生命周期安全的重新思考，呼吁建立更严格的数据
keywords: finetuning data stealing, open-source LLMs, backdoor training

title: Bias Similarity Measurement: A Black-Box Audit of Fairness Across LLMs
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) reproduce social biases, yet prevailing evaluations
score models in isolation, obscuring how biases persist across families and
releases. We introduce Bias Similarity Measurement (BSM), which treats fairness
as a relational property between models, unifying scalar, distributional, behavioral,
and representational signals into a single similarity space. Evaluating 30
LLMs on 1M+ prompts, we find that instruction tuning primarily enforces abstention
rather than altering internal representations; small models gain little accuracy
and can become less fair under forced choice; and open-weight models can match
or exceed proprietary systems. Family signatures diverge: Gemma favors refusal,
LLaMA 3.1 approaches neutrality with fewer refusals, and converges toward
abstention-heavy behavior overall. Counterintuitively, Gemma 3 Instruct matches
GPT-4–level fairness at far lower cost, whereas Gemini’s heavy abstention suppresses
utility. Beyond these findings, BSM offers an auditing workflow for procurement,
regression testing, and lineage screening, and extends naturally to code
and multilingual settings. Our results reframe fairness not as isolated scores but
as comparative bias similarity, enabling systematic auditing of LLM ecosystems.
Code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/bias_llm-0A8E.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 评估大语言模型（LLM）的公平性时，现有方法仅孤立评估单个模型，无法揭示不同模型间偏见的相似性与传播规律。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有评估方式忽略模型间的偏见关联，导致无法识别偏见在模型家族或版本迭代中的延续与演化。
  - 偏见的“传播”可能影响采购决策、系统安全与合规性，亟需一种可比较、可审计的公平性分析工具。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出 Bias Similarity Measurement (BSM)：将公平性视为模型间的“关系属性”，整合多种偏见信号（如响应分布、行为模式、表征差异）到统一相似性空间。
  - 通过黑盒方式分析模型输出，无需访问内部参数或训练数据，适用于闭源模型。
  - 基于百万级提示（prompt）测试，计算模型间偏见相似度，构建“偏见指纹”图谱。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 指出指令微调主要促使模型“拒绝回答”而非真正消除偏见。
  - 小模型在强制选择下公平性反而下降，开放权重模型可媲美甚至超越闭源系统。
  - 不同模型家族有独特“偏见签名”：Gemma 倾向拒绝，LLaMA 3.1 更中立，整体趋向回避。
  - BSM 可用于模型采购、回归测试、版本追踪等实际场景，支持代码与多语言扩展。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将公平性从“单模型评分”转向“模型间偏见相似性”分析，实现系统性审计。
  - 黑盒设计使 BSM 适用于闭源模型，具备广泛适用性。
  - 提出统一相似性空间整合多维度偏见信号，提升评估的全面性与可解释性。

title: Breaking and Fixing Defenses Against Control Flow Hijacking in Multi-Agent Systems
abstract:
Control-flow hijacking attacks manipulate orchestration mechanisms in multi-agent systems into performing unsafe actions that compromise the system and exfiltrate sensitive information.  Recently proposed defenses, such as LlamaFirewall, rely on alignment checks of inter-agent communications to ensure that all agent invocations are "related to" and "likely to further" the original objective.   

We start by demonstrating control-flow hijacking attacks that evade these defenses even if alignment checks are performed by advanced LLMs.  We argue that the safety and functionality objectives of multi-agent systems fundamentally conflict with each other.  This conflict is exacerbated by the brittle definitions of "alignment" and the checkers' incomplete visibility into the execution context.

We then propose, implement, and evaluate ControlValve, a new defense based on the principles of control-flow integrity and least privilege.  ControlValve (1) generates permitted control-flow graphs for multi-agent systems, and (2) enforces that all executions comply with these graphs, along with contextual rules (generated in a zero-shot manner) for each agent invocation.
explain_zh:
- We demonstrate that ControlValve is effective against both known and novel control-flow hijacking attacks, including those that exploit LLM-based alignment checkers.
- We evaluate ControlValve on a suite of 12 benchmark tasks, showing that it maintains high functionality (94.2% task success rate) while providing strong safety guarantees.

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 防止多智能体系统中的“控制流劫持”攻击，即攻击者通过操纵智能体间的协作流程，让系统执行危险操作或泄露敏感信息。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有防御方法（如 LlamaFirewall）依赖大模型检查智能体通信是否“相关”或“有助于原目标”，但这些检查容易被绕过。  
- 安全与功能之间存在根本矛盾：过于严格的检查会误杀正常行为，而宽松的检查则无法防御攻击。  
- 现有方法对“对齐”的定义模糊，且检查器无法看到完整的执行上下文。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出 ControlValve，基于“控制流完整性”和“最小权限”原则设计。  
- 预先生成每个任务的“允许的控制流图”（即智能体调用的合法路径）。  
- 执行时强制所有操作必须符合该图，并结合零样本生成的上下文规则进行动态验证。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- ControlValve 能有效防御已知和新型控制流劫持攻击，包括针对大模型检查器的攻击。  
- 在12个基准任务中，保持94.2%的任务成功率，说明其在保障安全的同时几乎不影响系统功能。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将“控制流完整性”思想引入多智能体系统安全，替代依赖大模型语义判断的防御方式。  
- 用可验证的图结构和零样本上下文规则实现更鲁棒、更透明的安全控制。  
- 在不牺牲功能的前提下，显著提升对复杂攻击的防御能力。

title: Buffer Matters: Unleashing the Power of Off-Policy Reinforcement Learning in Large Language Model Reasoning
abstract: Traditional on-policy Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) frameworks suffer from experience waste and reward homogeneity, which directly hinders learning efficiency on difficult samples during large language models post-training. In this paper, we introduce Batch Adaptation Policy Optimization (BAPO), an off-policy RLVR framework to improve the data efficiency in large language models post-training. It dynamically selects training batches by re-evaluating historically difficult samples and reusing high-quality ones, while holding a lower bound guarantee for policy improvement. Extensive experiments further demonstrate that BAPO achieves an average 12.5\% improvement over GRPO across mathematics, planning, and visual reasoning tasks. Crucially, BAPO successfully resolves 40.7\% of problems that base models consistently fail to solve.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 提升大语言模型在后训练阶段的强化学习效率，特别是对困难样本的学习能力。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）方法是“在线”的，会浪费大量历史经验，且奖励分布单一，难以有效训练模型处理复杂或困难的问题。
  - 大语言模型在数学、规划、视觉推理等任务中常遇到“死胡同”式难题，现有方法难以突破。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出BAPO：一种离线强化学习框架，允许重用历史经验，避免“用完即弃”。
  - 核心机制：动态筛选训练批次，重新评估过去难以解决的样本，并优先使用高质量（高奖励）的历史样本。
  - 保证策略改进下界，确保训练过程稳定且有效。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在数学、规划、视觉推理任务中，BAPO比GRPO平均提升12.5%。
  - 成功解决40.7%的基线模型始终无法解决的问题。
  - 适用于需要复杂推理的大语言模型后训练场景。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）

title: Can LLMs Reason Soundly in Law? Auditing Inference Patterns for Legal Judgment
abstract: This paper presents a method to analyze the inference patterns used by Large Language Models (LLMs) for judgment in a case study on legal LLMs, so as to identify potential incorrect representations of the LLM, according to human domain knowledge. Unlike traditional evaluations on language generation results, we propose to evaluate the correctness of the  detailed inference patterns of an LLM behind its seemingly correct outputs.  To this end, we quantify the interactions between input phrases used by the LLM as primitive inference patterns, because recent theoretical achievements have proven several mathematical guarantees of the faithfulness of the interaction-based explanation.  We design a set of metrics to evaluate the detailed inference patterns of LLMs. Experiments show that even when the language generation results appear correct, a significant portion of the inference patterns used by the LLM for the legal judgment may represent misleading or irrelevant logic.
explain_zh:
- 问题：LLMs在法律判决中是否能进行合理推理？
- 动机：传统评估只看输出结果，但即使输出正确，推理过程可能错误。
- 方法：通过量化LLM推理中输入片段间的交互，分析其推理模式。
- 结论：许多看似正确的判决，其推理过程存在误导或无关逻辑。
- 创新点：首次从推理模式角度评估LLM，引入交互量化与可解释性指标。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 评估大语言模型（LLM）在法律判决任务中是否真正进行了合理推理。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 传统评估只看输出结果是否正确，但LLM可能“猜对”答案，而推理过程是错误或无关的。  
- 法律决策需逻辑严谨，不能仅靠表面正确，必须审查推理过程是否可信。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出一种分析LLM推理路径的新方法：聚焦其内部使用的“输入片段”之间的交互。  
- 利用理论保证的交互解释技术，量化这些片段如何共同支撑结论。  
- 设计可解释性指标，系统评估推理链条的合理性与相关性。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 即使LLM输出的法律判决看似正确，其背后的推理模式中仍有大量误导性或无关逻辑。  
- 说明仅依赖生成结果的评估存在严重风险，尤其在法律等高风险领域。  
- 该方法可有效识别“表面正确、实则错误”的推理模式。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次从“推理模式”角度评估LLM，突破传统“结果导向”评价范式。  
- 引入基于交互的量化分析，结合可解释性理论，实现对推理过程的可度量审计。  
- 为法律AI的可信性评估提供新工具，推动AI在关键领域的负责任应用。

title: Can LLMs Refuse Questions They Do Not Know? Measuring Knowledge-Aware Refusal in Factual Tasks
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) should refuse to answer questions beyond their knowledge. This capability, which we term knowledge-aware refusal, is crucial for factual reliability. However, existing metrics fail to faithfully measure this ability. On the one hand, simple refusal-based metrics are biased by refusal rates and yield inconsistent scores when models exhibit different refusal tendencies. On the other hand, existing calibration metrics are proxy-based, capturing the performance of auxiliary calibration processes rather than the model’s actual refusal behavior. In this work, we propose the Refusal Index (RI), a principled metric that measures how accurately LLMs refuse questions they do not know. We define RI as Spearman’s rank correlation between refusal probability and error probability. To make RI practically measurable, we design a lightweight two-pass evaluation method that efficiently estimates RI from observed refusal rates across two standard evaluation runs. Extensive experiments across 16 models and 5 datasets demonstrate that RI accurately quantifies a model’s intrinsic knowledge-aware refusal capability in factual tasks. Notably, RI remains stable across different refusal rates and provides consistent model rankings independent of a model’s overall accuracy and refusal rates. More importantly, RI provides insight into an important but previously overlooked aspect of LLM factuality: while LLMs achieve high accuracy on factual tasks, their refusal behavior can be unreliable and fragile. This finding highlights the need to complement traditional accuracy metrics with the Refusal Index for comprehensive factuality evaluation.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 评估大语言模型在不知道答案时是否能正确拒绝回答的能力。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有评估方法要么受拒绝率干扰，要么只衡量辅助校准过程，无法真实反映模型“知道就答、不知道就拒”的能力。
  - 模型高准确率不等于可靠拒绝，实际中可能“装懂”乱答，影响事实可靠性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出“拒绝指数”（Refusal Index, RI），基于斯皮尔曼相关性，衡量拒绝概率与错误概率的相关性。
  - 设计轻量级两阶段评估法：通过两次标准测试，估算模型在不同问题上的拒绝率与错误率，进而计算RI。
  - 不依赖模型内部结构，仅用外部行为数据，可广泛适配各类模型。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - RI能稳定、一致地评估模型的“知之为知之”能力，不受拒绝率或准确率影响。
  - 在16个模型、5个数据集上验证有效，揭示多数模型拒绝行为不可靠。
  - 适用于所有事实类任务的评估，尤其适合高风险场景（如医疗、法律）。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次提出基于真实拒绝行为的可量化指标（RI），摆脱对辅助校准的依赖。
  - 两阶段评估法实现高效、低成本测量，无需修改模型或额外训练。
  - 揭示“高准确率≠可靠拒绝”的关键问题，推动事实性评估从“答对”转向“知错就拒”。

title: Developmental Federated Tuning: A Cognitive-Inspired Paradigm for Efficient LLM Adaptation
abstract: Federated fine-tuning enables Large Language Models (LLMs) to adapt to downstream tasks while preserving data privacy, but its resource-intensive nature severely limits deployment on edge devices. In this paper, we introduce Developmental Federated Tuning (DevFT), a resource-efficient approach inspired by cognitive development that progressively builds a powerful LLM from a compact foundation. DevFT decomposes the fine-tuning process into developmental stages, each optimizing a submodel with increasing parameter capacity. Knowledge acquired in earlier stages is transferred to subsequent submodels, providing optimized initialization parameters that prevent convergence to local minima and accelerate training. This paradigm mirrors human learning, gradually constructing a comprehensive knowledge structure while refining existing skills. To efficiently build stage-specific submodels, DevFT introduces deconfliction-guided layer grouping and differential-based layer fusion to distill essential information and construct representative layers. Evaluations across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that DevFT significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving up to $\textbf{4.59$\times$}$ faster convergence, $\textbf{10.67$\times$}$ reduction in communication overhead, and $\textbf{9.07}$\% average performance improvement, while maintaining compatibility with existing federated fine-tuning approaches.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 实现高效、低资源消耗的联邦微调，使大型语言模型能在边缘设备上安全适应下游任务。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统联邦微调计算与通信开销大，难以在资源受限的边缘设备上部署。
  - 人类学习是渐进式发展的，现有方法未借鉴这种高效学习机制。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 将模型微调划分为多个“发展阶段”，每个阶段训练一个参数量递增的子模型。
  - 前一阶段的知识通过优化初始化参数传递给下一阶段，避免陷入局部最优。
  - 引入“去冲突引导的层分组”和“基于差分的层融合”技术，高效提取关键信息并构建代表性层。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个基准测试中，相比SOTA方法：收敛速度提升4.59倍，通信开销减少10.67倍，平均性能提升9.07%。
  - 适用于现有联邦微调框架，兼容性强，适合边缘设备部署。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将“认知发展”理念引入联邦微调，提出分阶段渐进式模型构建范式。
  - 设计了去冲突引导的层分组与差分融合机制，实现高效知识迁移与模型压缩。

title: Diversity-Enhanced Reasoning for Subjective Questions
abstract:
Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) with long chain-of-thought capabilities, optimized via reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), excel at **objective reasoning** tasks like mathematical problem solving and code generation.
However, RLVR is known for degrading generation diversity, which causes LRMs to fall short on **subjective reasoning** that has multiple answers depending on different role perspectives.
While recent studies recognize the importance of diversity-enhanced training in objective reasoning, limited attention has been given to subjective tasks.
In this paper, we find that subjective reasoning can be improved by introducing perspective diversity and token-level diversity, with the former one providing a coherent scaffolding anchored to a real-world stakeholder group and the latter one broadening the answer search space.
We propose MultiRole-R1, a diversity-enhanced training framework featuring an unsupervised data construction pipeline that synthesizes reasoning chains incorporating various role perspectives.
It also employs reinforcement learning via Group Relative Policy Optimization with reward shaping, taking diversity as a reward signal in addition to verifiable reward.
Training on subjective tasks solely, MultiRole-R1 increases the in-domain and out-of-domain accuracy by 14.1% and 7.64%, and even enhances the performance on advanced math reasoning such as AIME 2024.
We further show that diversity is a more consistent indicator of accuracy than reasoning length.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 提升大模型在主观问题上的推理能力，使其能生成多样且合理的多角度回答。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有强化学习优化的大模型（如RLVR）在客观任务上表现优秀，但缺乏多样性，难以应对主观问题（如“是否该建新机场？”）——这类问题本就无唯一正确答案，需从不同角色视角出发。
  - 当前研究多关注客观任务的多样性，忽视了主观任务中“视角多样性”的关键作用。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建无监督数据生成流程：自动合成包含不同角色视角（如居民、政府、环保组织）的推理链。
  - 提出MultiRole-R1框架：结合**角色多样性**（提供结构化视角）与**token级多样性**（扩大答案搜索空间）。
  - 使用**组相对策略优化（Group Relative Policy Optimization）+ 奖励塑形**，将多样性作为奖励信号之一，与可验证奖励共同训练模型。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在主观任务上，模型准确率提升14.1%（域内）和7.64%（域外）。
  - 即使在高级数学推理（如AIME 2024）中也表现更好，说明多样性提升具有泛化能力。
  - 多样性比推理长度更能稳定预测模型准确性。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次系统性将“角色视角多样性”引入主观推理训练，构建有结构的多角度推理框架。
  - 提出以多样性为显式奖励信号的强化学习机制，实现“好答案”与“好多样性”的协同优化。

title: Downgrade to Upgrade: Optimizer Simplification Enhances Robustness in LLM Unlearning
abstract: Large language model (LLM) unlearning aims to surgically remove the influence of undesired data or knowledge from an existing model while preserving its utility on unrelated tasks. This paradigm has shown promise in addressing privacy and safety concerns. However, recent findings reveal that unlearning effects are often *fragile*: post-unlearning manipulations such as weight quantization or fine-tuning can quickly neutralize the intended forgetting. Prior efforts to improve robustness primarily reformulate unlearning objectives by explicitly assuming the role of vulnerability sources.  In this work, we take a different perspective by investigating the role of the *optimizer*, independent of unlearning objectives and formulations, in shaping unlearning robustness. We show that the "*grade*" of the optimizer, defined by the level of information it exploits, ranging from zeroth-order (gradient-free) to first-order (gradient-based) to second-order (Hessian-based), is tightly linked to the resilience of unlearning. Surprisingly, we find that downgrading the optimizer, such as using zeroth-order methods or compressed-gradient variants (*e.g.,* gradient sign-based optimizers), often leads to stronger robustness. While these optimizers produce noisier and less precise updates, they encourage convergence to harder-to-disturb basins in the loss landscape, thereby resisting post-training perturbations. By connecting zeroth-order methods with randomized smoothing, we further highlight their natural advantage for robust unlearning.  Motivated by these insights, we propose a *hybrid optimizer* that combines first-order and zeroth-order updates, preserving unlearning efficacy while enhancing robustness. Extensive experiments on the MUSE and WMDP benchmarks, across multiple LLM unlearning algorithms, validate that our approach achieves more resilient forgetting without sacrificing unlearning quality.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何让大模型“遗忘”特定数据后，仍能抵抗后续操作（如量化、微调）导致的遗忘失效。  

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有遗忘方法在微调或量化后容易失效，说明遗忘效果“脆弱”；  
  - 以往方法聚焦于修改遗忘目标，但忽略了优化器本身对鲁棒性的影响。  

- 方法是什么？  
  - 发现优化器的“等级”（零阶/一阶/二阶）影响遗忘鲁棒性；  
  - 零阶优化器（如基于梯度符号的方法）虽更新粗糙，但能引导模型进入更稳定的损失谷底；  
  - 提出混合优化器：结合一阶与零阶更新，兼顾遗忘效果与抗干扰能力。  

- 结论是什么？  
  - 使用简化优化器（如零阶）可显著提升遗忘鲁棒性；  
  - 混合优化器在MUSE和WMDP数据集上验证有效，既保持遗忘质量，又抵抗后处理干扰。  

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次揭示优化器等级对遗忘鲁棒性的关键作用；  
  - 提出“降级优化器反而更鲁棒”的反直觉思路；  
  - 设计混合优化器，实现遗忘效果与鲁棒性的平衡。

title: Dual-Space Smoothness for Robust and Balanced LLM Unlearning
abstract: As large language models evolve, Machine Unlearning has emerged to address growing concerns around user privacy, copyright infringement, and overall safety. Yet state-of-the-art (SOTA) unlearning methods often suffer from catastrophic forgetting and metric imbalance, for example, by over-optimizing one objective (e.g., unlearning effectiveness, utility preservation, or privacy protection) at the expense of others. In addition, small perturbations in the representation or parameter space can be exploited by relearn and jailbreak attacks. To address these challenges, we propose PRISM, a unified framework that enforces dual-space smoothness in representation and parameter spaces to improve robustness and balance unlearning metrics. PRISM consists of two smoothness optimization stages: (i) a representation space stage that employs a robustly trained probe to defend against jailbreak attacks, and (ii) a parameter-space stage that decouples retain–forget gradient conflicts, reduces imbalance, and smooths the parameter space to mitigate relearning attacks. Extensive experiments on WMDP and MUSE, across conversational-dialogue and continuous-text settings, show that PRISM outperforms SOTA baselines under multiple attacks while achieving a better balance among key metrics.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何在不损害模型性能的前提下，安全、高效地从大语言模型中移除特定数据，同时避免遗忘或隐私泄露。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有方法在“遗忘效果”“模型性能”“隐私保护”之间难以平衡，容易导致灾难性遗忘或被攻击者利用漏洞重新学习被删除数据。  
  - 小扰动可能引发“重学攻击”或“越狱攻击”，威胁模型安全性。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出PRISM框架，通过双空间平滑机制增强鲁棒性：  
    - 表示空间：用一个鲁棒探测器防御越狱攻击，确保隐藏层表示稳定。  
    - 参数空间：解耦保留与遗忘的梯度冲突，平滑参数更新路径，防止重学攻击。  
  - 分两阶段优化：先稳定表示，再优化参数，提升整体一致性。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 在WMDP和MUSE数据集上，PRISM在多种攻击下均优于SOTA方法。  
  - 实现了遗忘效果、模型性能与隐私保护之间的更好平衡。  
  - 适用于对话和连续文本场景。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次引入“双空间平滑”机制，同时保障表示与参数空间的稳定性。  
  - 通过梯度解耦缓解保留与遗忘任务的冲突，提升训练平衡性。  
  - 显著增强对重学和越狱攻击的防御能力，推动安全可遗忘的实用化。

title: EditLens: Quantifying the Extent of AI Editing in Text
abstract: A significant proportion of queries to large language models ask them to edit user-provided text, rather than generate new text from scratch. While previous work focuses on detecting fully AI-generated text, we demonstrate that AI-edited text is distinguishable from human-written and AI-generated text. First, we propose using lightweight similarity metrics to quantify the magnitude of AI editing present in a text given the original human-written text and validate these metrics with human annotators. Using these similarity metrics as intermediate supervision, we then train EditLens, a regression model that predicts the amount of AI editing present within a text. Our model achieves state-of-the-art performance on both binary (F1=94.7%) and ternary (F1=90.4%) classification tasks in distinguishing human, AI, and mixed writing. Not only do we show that AI-edited text can be detected, but also that the degree of change made by AI to human writing can be detected, which has implications for authorship attribution, education, and policy. Finally, as a case study, we use our model to analyze the effects of AI-edits applied by Grammarly, a popular writing assistance tool. To encourage further research, we commit to publicly releasing our models and dataset.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 大量用户使用AI工具对已有文本进行修改，但现有技术只关注“是否为AI生成”，忽视了“AI修改”这一常见场景。
  - AI编辑行为在教育、版权和学术诚信中影响重大，亟需可量化检测手段。

  - 用人工标注验证这些指标的有效性，作为中间监督信号。
  - 构建EditLens模型，基于相似度指标训练回归模型，预测AI编辑的“程度”。
  - 推理时输入原始文本与修改后文本，输出AI编辑强度得分。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）

title: Expressiveness of Multi-Neuron Convex Relaxations in Neural Network Certification
abstract:
Neural network certification methods heavily rely on convex relaxations to provide robustness guarantees. However, these relaxations are often imprecise: even the most accurate single-neuron relaxation is incomplete for general ReLU networks, a limitation known as the *single-neuron convex barrier*. While multi-neuron relaxations have been heuristically applied to address this issue, two central questions arise: (i) whether they overcome the convex barrier, and if not, (ii) whether they offer theoretical capabilities beyond those of single-neuron relaxations.
In this work, we present the first rigorous analysis of the expressiveness of multi-neuron relaxations. Perhaps surprisingly, we show that they are inherently incomplete, even when allocated sufficient resources to capture finitely many neurons and layers optimally. This result extends the single-neuron barrier to a *universal convex barrier* for neural network certification. 
On the positive side, we show that completeness can be achieved by either (i) augmenting the network with a polynomial number of carefully designed ReLU neurons or (ii) partitioning the input domain into convex sub-polytopes, thereby distinguishing multi-neuron relaxations from single-neuron ones which are unable to realize the former and have worse partition complexity for the latter.
Our findings establish a foundation for multi-neuron relaxations and point to new directions for certified robustness, including training methods tailored to multi-neuron relaxations and verification methods with multi-neuron relaxations as the main subroutine.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 神经网络认证中，多神经元凸松弛方法是否能突破单神经元松弛的理论局限（即“凸松弛障碍”）？

- 动机是什么？  
  - 单神经元松弛存在理论缺陷，无法完全保证神经网络的鲁棒性；尽管多神经元松弛被广泛尝试，但其理论能力尚不明确，亟需系统分析。  
  - 理解多神经元方法是否真正提升认证能力，对设计更可靠的AI安全验证方法至关重要。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 从理论角度严格分析多神经元凸松弛的表达能力，证明其在任意规模下仍存在固有不完整性。  
  - 提出两种实现完整性的替代方案：（1）在原网络中添加少量精心设计的ReLU神经元；（2）将输入空间划分为多个凸多面体子区域进行分别认证。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 多神经元松弛无法完全克服“凸松弛障碍”，存在普遍性理论局限。  
  - 但通过添加神经元或划分输入空间，可实现完整认证，且优于单神经元方法。  
  - 适用于ReLU网络的鲁棒性验证，为未来认证算法设计提供新思路。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次揭示多神经元凸松弛存在“普遍凸松弛障碍”，打破其“更优”的直觉。  
  - 提出两种可行的补救路径，为认证方法设计开辟新方向。  
  - 建立理论基础，推动认证技术从“近似”走向“完整”。

title: General Exploratory Bonus for Optimistic Exploration in RLHF
abstract: Optimistic exploration is central to improving sample efficiency in reinforcement learning with human feedback, yet existing exploratory bonus methods often fail to realize true optimism. We provide a theoretical analysis showing that current formulations, under KL or $\alpha$-divergence regularization, unintentionally bias exploration toward high-probability regions of the reference model, thereby reinforcing conservative behavior instead of promoting discovery of uncertain regions. To address this pitfall, we introduce the General Exploratory Bonus (GEB), a novel theoretical framework that provably satisfies the optimism principle. GEB counteracts divergence-induced bias via reference-dependent reward regulation and unifies prior heuristic bonuses as special cases, while extending naturally across the full $\alpha$-divergence family. Empirically, GEB consistently outperforms baselines on alignment tasks across multiple divergence settings and large language model backbones. These results demonstrate that GEB offers both a principled and practical solution for optimistic exploration in RLHF.
explain_zh:
---

- **解决的问题是什么？**  
  - 如何在基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）中实现真正的“乐观探索”，以提升样本效率。

- **动机是什么？**  
  - 现有探索奖励方法在理论上无法真正实现乐观探索，反而会偏向参考模型已知的高概率区域，导致行为保守，难以发现新且有价值的策略。  
  - 这限制了RLHF在少样本场景下的性能，亟需一种能真正鼓励探索未知区域的方法。

- **方法是什么？**  
  - 提出通用探索奖励（GEB），一种基于参考模型的奖励调节机制，通过动态调整探索奖励来抵消由KL或α-散度正则化带来的偏差。  
  - GEB在理论上保证了“乐观性”——即鼓励探索策略分布中不确定性高的区域。  
  - 该框架可统一现有启发式探索奖励，并适用于所有α-散度类型，具有良好的泛化能力。

- **结论是什么？**  
  - 在多个大语言模型和不同散度设置下，GEB显著优于现有基线方法，在对齐任务中提升样本效率。  
  - 证明了其在真实RLHF场景中的有效性与实用性。

- **创新点是什么？**  
  - 首次从理论层面揭示现有方法的“伪乐观”问题，提出真正满足乐观原则的GEB框架。  
  - 通过参考模型依赖的奖励调节，实现对探索偏差的主动纠正，兼具理论严谨性与工程实用性。

title: Human-LLM Collaborative Feature Engineering for Tabular Data
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to automate feature engineering in tabular learning. Given task-specific information, LLMs can propose diverse feature transformation operations to enhance downstream model performance. However, current approaches typically assign the LLM as a black-box optimizer, responsible for both proposing and selecting operations based solely on its internal heuristics, which often lack calibrated estimations of operation utility and consequently lead to repeated exploration of low-yield operations without a principled strategy for prioritizing promising directions. In this paper, we propose a human–LLM collaborative feature engineering framework for tabular learning. We begin by decoupling the transformation operation proposal and selection processes, where LLMs are used solely to generate operation candidates, while the selection is guided by explicitly modeling the utility and uncertainty of each proposed operation. Since accurate utility estimation can be difficult especially in the early rounds of feature engineering, we design a mechanism within the framework that selectively elicits and incorporates human expert preference feedback—comparing which operations are more promising—into the selection process to help identify more effective operations.
Our evaluations on both the synthetic study and the real user study demonstrate that the proposed framework improves feature engineering performance across a variety of tabular datasets and reduces users’ cognitive load during the feature engineering process.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在表格数据特征工程中，让大语言模型（LLM）更高效、更智能地提出和选择特征转换操作，避免盲目尝试低效方案。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 当前LLM在特征工程中被当作“黑箱优化器”，仅靠内部直觉提出并选择操作，缺乏对操作价值的准确判断，导致重复尝试无效操作，效率低下。
  - 人工专家虽有判断力，但需投入大量精力，难以持续参与。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 将特征工程拆分为两个阶段：LLM只负责生成候选操作，不参与选择。
  - 引入一个可解释的“评估模块”，对每个候选操作估计其潜在价值（utility）和不确定性（uncertainty）。
  - 在早期阶段，若评估不明确，主动向人类专家提问：在两个候选操作中，哪个更可能有效？——通过对比反馈提升判断准确性。
  - 逐步筛选出最有潜力的操作，减少无效尝试。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在合成数据和真实用户实验中，该框架显著提升特征工程效果，优于纯LLM或纯人工方法。
  - 显著降低用户认知负担，使专家能更聚焦于关键决策。
  - 适用于多种表格数据场景，尤其适合专家资源有限的场景。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将LLM与人类专家的协作机制系统化引入特征工程，实现“LLM出主意，人来挑优”。
  - 提出“评估+人类反馈”双机制，用对比提问方式高效获取专家偏好，提升早期决策质量。
  - 通过解耦操作生成与选择，使整个流程更透明、可控、可解释。

title: In Agents We Trust, but Who Do Agents Trust? Latent Preferences Steer LLM Generations
abstract:
Large Language Model (LLM) based agents are increasingly being deployed as user-friendly front-ends on online platforms, where they filter, prioritize, and recommend information retrieved from the platforms' back-end databases or via web search. In these scenarios, LLM agents act as decision assistants, drawing users' attention to particular instances of retrieved information at the expense of others. While much prior work has focused on biases in the information LLMs themselves generate, less attention has been paid to the factors and mechanisms that determine how LLMs select and present information to users.

We hypothesize that when information is attributed to specific sources (e.g., particular publishers, journals, or platforms), LLMs will exhibit systematic latent source preferences. That is, they will prioritize information from some sources over others based on attributes such as the sources' brand identity, reputation, or perceived expertise, encoded within their parametric knowledge. Through controlled experiments on twelve LLMs from six model providers, spanning both synthetic and real-world tasks including news recommendation, research paper selection, and choosing e-commerce platforms, we find that several models consistently exhibit strong and predictable source preferences. These preferences are sensitive to contextual framing, can outweigh the influence of content itself, and persist despite explicit prompting to avoid them. They also help explain phenomena such as the observed left-leaning skew in news recommendations, which arises from higher trust in certain sources rather than the content itself. Our findings advocate for deeper investigation into the origins of these preferences during pretraining, fine-tuning and instruction tuning, as well as for mechanisms that provide users with transparency and control over the biases guiding LLM-powered agents.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - LLM代理在推荐信息时，会无意识地偏好某些信息来源，导致推荐结果存在隐蔽偏见。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有研究多关注LLM生成内容的偏见，但忽视了其在筛选和推荐信息时对来源的隐性偏好。
  - 这种偏好可能影响用户决策，如新闻推荐偏向特定媒体，但原因不明。
  - 需要揭示LLM“信任谁”的内在机制，以提升透明度和公平性。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 在12个主流LLM上设计控制实验，测试其在新闻、论文、电商等任务中对不同来源的推荐倾向。
  - 通过改变来源名称（如“知名媒体” vs “小众平台”）但保持内容一致，观察推荐差异。
  - 分析偏好是否受上下文影响，是否能被提示抑制，以及是否与模型来源或训练阶段相关。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 多个LLM表现出稳定、可预测的来源偏好，且偏好常强于内容本身。
  - 偏好受上下文影响，难以通过提示消除，可能源于预训练或指令微调中的隐性学习。
  - 可解释部分新闻推荐的“左倾”现象，源于对特定媒体的信任而非内容立场。
  - 适用于所有依赖LLM做信息筛选的场景，如推荐系统、搜索代理、智能客服。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次系统揭示LLM在信息筛选中存在“隐性来源偏好”这一新型偏见。
  - 提出“信任来源”作为理解LLM决策的新视角，超越“内容偏见”范畴。
  - 强调需从预训练和微调阶段追溯偏见根源，推动可解释性与用户控制机制发展。
